message.content:
Search thoroughly in both FormalConjectures/ and FormalConjecturesForMathlib/ for any code related to:
1. Sumsets (restricted or unrestricted) - search for "sumset", "Sumset", "restrictedSum", "hat"
2. Additive combinatorics in finite fields - search for "ZMod", "additive", "Cauchy-Davenport", "Heilbronn"
3. Finset operations that combine elements pairwise - search for "biUnion.*image", "pairwise sum"

Report all findings with file paths and line numbers. Be very thorough - check multiple naming conventions.

    19‚Üí/--
    20‚ÜíA perfect difference set modulo `n` is a set `D` such that the map `(a, b) ‚Ü¶ a - b` from
    21‚Üí`D.offDiag` to `{x : ZMod n | x ‚Č† 0}` is a bijection.

    16‚Üíimport FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Algebra.Group.Indicator

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Convolution of Functions on ‚Ąē
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíThis file defines the sum (`‚ąó`) convolution of functions `‚Ąē ‚Üí R`.
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí## Main Definitions
    25‚Üí* `AdditiveCombinatorics.sumConv`: The sum convolution `f ‚ąó g`.
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí## Notation
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí* `f ‚ąó g` for `sumConv f g`.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí## TODO
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí* `f ‚ąė g` for `diffConv f g`.

    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace AdditiveCombinatorics
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üíopen Finset Classical Set
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üívariable {R : Type*} [Semiring R]
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- The sum convolution of two functions `f, g : ‚Ąē ‚Üí R`, also known as the Cauchy product.
    45‚Üí`(f ‚ąó g) n = ‚ąĎ_{a+b=n} f(a)g(b)`. -/
    46‚Üídef sumConv (f g : ‚Ąē ‚Üí R) (n : ‚Ąē) : R := ‚ąĎ p ‚ąą antidiagonal n, f p.1 * g p.2
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üíinfixl:70 " ‚ąó " => sumConv
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- The number of sum representations is the sum convolution of `A`'s indicator
    51‚Üífunction with itself: $1_A\ast 1_A(n)$. -/
    52‚Üínoncomputable def sumRep (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē := (ūĚüô_A ‚ąó ūĚüô_A)
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí@[simp]
    56‚Üílemma sumRep_def (A : Set ‚Ąē) (n : ‚Ąē) :
    57‚Üí    sumRep A n = ((antidiagonal n).filter (fun (p : ‚Ąē √ó ‚Ąē) ‚Ü¶ p.1 ‚ąą A ‚ąß p.2 ‚ąą A)).card := by
    58‚Üí  simp only [sumRep, sumConv, indicatorOne, indicator, mul_ite, mul_one, mul_zero]
    59‚Üí  push_cast [‚Üź ite_and, card_filter, and_comm]
    60‚Üí  congr!
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üíopen PowerSeries
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üítheorem sumRep_eq_powerSeries_coeff (A : Set ‚Ąē) (n : ‚Ąē) : (sumRep A n : ‚Ąē) =
    65‚Üí    ((PowerSeries.mk (ūĚüô_A)) * (PowerSeries.mk (ūĚüô_A)) : PowerSeries ‚Ąē).coeff n := by
    66‚Üí  simp [sumRep, sumConv, indicatorOne, indicator, PowerSeries.coeff_mul, PowerSeries.coeff_mk]
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üíend AdditiveCombinatorics

    16‚Üíimport FormalConjecturesForMathlib.Order.Filter.Cofinite

    22‚Üíopen Filter

    24‚Üí
    25‚Üínamespace Set
    26‚Üívariable {M : Type*} [CommMonoid M] {A : Set M} {m n : ‚Ąē}
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is a multiplicative basis of order `n` if any element `a : M`
    29‚Üícan be expressed as a product of `n` elements lying in `A`. -/
    30‚Üí@[to_additive
    31‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an additive basis of order `n` if for any element
    32‚Üí`a : M`, it can be expressed as a sum of `n` elements lying in `A`. -/]
    33‚Üídef IsMulBasisOfOrder (A : Set M) (n : ‚Ąē) : Prop := ‚ąÄ a, a ‚ąą A ^ n
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/-- A multiplicative basis of some order. -/
    36‚Üí@[to_additive /-- An additive basis of some order. -/]
    37‚Üídef IsMulBasis (A : Set M) : Prop := ‚ąÉ n, A.IsMulBasisOfOrder n
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí@[to_additive]
    40‚Üílemma IsMulBasisOfOrder.isMulBasis (hA : A.IsMulBasisOfOrder n) : A.IsMulBasis := ‚ü®n, hA‚ü©
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí@[to_additive]
    43‚Üílemma isMulBasisOfOrder_iff :
    44‚Üí    A.IsMulBasisOfOrder n ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ a, ‚ąÉ (f : Fin n ‚Üí M) (_ : ‚ąÄ i, f i ‚ąą A), ‚ąŹ i, f i = a := by
    45‚Üí  have := Set.mem_finset_prod (t := .univ) (f := fun _ : Fin n ‚Ü¶ A)
    46‚Üí  simp_all [IsMulBasisOfOrder]
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- No set is a multiplicative basis of order `0`. -/
    49‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp) /-- No set is an additive basis of order `0`. -/]
    50‚Üílemma not_isMulBasisOfOrder_zero [Nontrivial M] : ¬¨A.IsMulBasisOfOrder 0 := by
    51‚Üí  simp [isMulBasisOfOrder_iff, eq_comm, exists_ne]
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    54‚Üílemma isMulBasisOfOrder_one_iff : A.IsMulBasisOfOrder 1 ‚ÜĒ A = univ := by
    55‚Üí  simp [IsMulBasisOfOrder, eq_univ_iff_forall]
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is a multiplicative basis of order `2` if every `a : M` belongs to `A * A`. -/
    58‚Üí@[to_additive
    59‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an additive basis of order `2` if every `a : M` belongs to `A + A`. -/]
    60‚Üílemma isMulBasisOfOrder_two_iff : A.IsMulBasisOfOrder 2 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ a, a ‚ąą A * A := by
    61‚Üí  simp [IsMulBasisOfOrder, pow_two]
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `n` if the elements that can
    64‚Üíbe expressed as a product of `n` elements lying in `A` is cofinite. -/
    65‚Üí@[to_additive
    66‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `n` if the elements that
    67‚Üícan be expressed as a sum of `n` elements lying in `A` is cofinite. -/]
    68‚Üídef IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder (A : Set M) (n : ‚Ąē) : Prop := ‚ąÄŠ∂† a in cofinite, a ‚ąą A ^ n
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üí/-- An asymptotic multiplicative basis of some order. -/
    71‚Üí@[to_additive /-- An asymptotic additive basis of some order. -/]
    72‚Üídef IsAsymptoticMulBasis (A : Set M) : Prop := ‚ąÉ n, A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üí@[to_additive]
    75‚Üílemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.isAsymptoticMulBasis (hA : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n) :
    76‚Üí    A.IsAsymptoticMulBasis := ‚ü®n, hA‚ü©
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí@[to_additive]
    79‚Üílemma IsMulBasisOfOrder.isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder (hA : IsMulBasisOfOrder A n) :
    80‚Üí    A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n := .of_forall hA
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí@[to_additive]
    83‚Üílemma IsMulBasis.isAsymptoticMulBasis (hA : IsMulBasis A) : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasis := by
    84‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®n, hn‚ü© := hA; exact ‚ü®n, hn.isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder‚ü©
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `n` if a cofinite set of
    87‚Üí`a : M` can be written as `a = a‚āĀ * ... * a‚āô`, where each `aŠĶĘ ‚ąą A`. -/
    88‚Üí@[to_additive
    89‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `n` if a cofinite set of
    90‚Üí`a : M` can be written as `a = a‚āĀ + ... + a‚āô`, where each `aŠĶĘ ‚ąą A`. -/]
    91‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_iff_prod :
    92‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄŠ∂† a in cofinite, ‚ąÉ (f : Fin n ‚Üí M) (_ : ‚ąÄ i, f i ‚ąą A),
    93‚Üí      ‚ąŹ i, f i = a := by
    94‚Üí  have := Set.mem_finset_prod (t := .univ) (f := fun _ : Fin n ‚Ü¶ A)
    95‚Üí  simp_all [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder]
    96‚Üí
    97‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `2` if a cofinite set of
    98‚Üí`a : M` belongs to `A * A`. -/
    99‚Üí@[to_additive
   100‚Üí/-- A set `A : Set M` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `2` if a cofinite set of
   101‚Üí`a : M` belongs to `A + A`. -/]
   102‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_two_iff :
   103‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 2 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄŠ∂† a in cofinite, a ‚ąą A * A := by
   104‚Üí  simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, pow_two]
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
   107‚Üíprotected lemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.of_finite [Finite M] :
   108‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n := by simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder]
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí/-- If `M` is infinite, then no set `A` is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `0`. -/
   111‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)
   112‚Üí/-- If `M` is infinite, then no set `A` is an asymptotic additive basis of order `0`. -/]
   113‚Üílemma not_isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_zero [Infinite M] : ¬¨IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 0 := by
   114‚Üí  simpa [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, ‚Üź not_infinite] using Set.infinite_of_finite_compl (by simp)
   115‚Üí
   116‚Üí@[to_additive]
   117‚Üíprotected lemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.ne_zero [Infinite M]
   118‚Üí    (hA : IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n) : n ‚Č† 0 := by rintro rfl; simp at hA
   119‚Üí
   120‚Üí@[to_additive]
   121‚Üíprotected lemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.nonempty [Infinite M]
   122‚Üí    (hA : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n) : A.Nonempty := by
   123‚Üí  by_contra!
   124‚Üí  simp [this, IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, hA.ne_zero, finite_univ_iff,
   125‚Üí    _root_.Infinite.not_finite] at hA
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üí/-- `A : Set M` is an asymptotic basis of order one iff it is cofinite. -/
   128‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)
   129‚Üí/--`A : Set M` is an asymptotic basis of order one iff it is cofinite. -/]
   130‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_one_iff : IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 1 ‚ÜĒ AŠ∂ú.Finite := by
   131‚Üí  simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, compl_def]
   132‚Üí
   133‚Üívariable [LinearOrder M] [LocallyFiniteOrder M] [OrderBot M]
   134‚Üí
   135‚Üí@[to_additive]
   136‚Üílemma IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder.mono [CanonicallyOrderedMul M] [IsCancelMul M] (hmn : m ‚Č§ n)
   137‚Üí    (hA : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder m) : A.IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder n := by
   138‚Üí  cases finite_or_infinite M
   139‚Üí  ¬∑ simp
   140‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®a, ha‚ü© := hA.nonempty
   141‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®n, rfl‚ü© := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_le hmn
   142‚Üí  refine ((hA.smul_set (a := a ^ n)).union <| finite_Iio <| a ^ n).subset ?_
   143‚Üí  have : a ^ n ‚ÄĘ (A ^ m)Š∂ú = (a ^ n ‚ÄĘ A ^ m)Š∂ú \ Iio (a ^ n) := by
   144‚Üí    ext x
   145‚Üí    constructor
   146‚Üí    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®y, hy, rfl‚ü©
   147‚Üí      simpa
   148‚Üí    ¬∑ simp only [mem_diff, mem_compl_iff, mem_smul_set, smul_eq_mul, not_exists, not_and, mem_Iio,
   149‚Üí        not_lt, le_iff_exists_mul, and_imp, forall_exists_index]
   150‚Üí      rintro hx y rfl
   151‚Üí      exact ‚ü®y, fun hy ‚Ü¶ hx _ hy rfl, rfl‚ü©
   152‚Üí  simp only [m.add_comm, pow_add, setOf_mem_eq, this, diff_union_self, subset_def, mem_compl_iff,
   153‚Üí    mem_union, mem_Iio]
   154‚Üí  rintro b hb
   155‚Üí  contrapose! hb
   156‚Üí  exact smul_set_subset_mul (pow_mem_pow ha) hb.1
   157‚Üí
   158‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `1`
   159‚Üíif it contains an infinite tail of elements. -/
   160‚Üí@[to_additive
   161‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic additive basis of order `1`
   162‚Üíif it contains an infinite tail of consecutive naturals. -/]
   163‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_one_iff_Ioi :
   164‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 1 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ a, .Ioi a ‚äÜ A := by
   165‚Üí  simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, cofinite_hasBasis_Ioi.eventually_iff, Set.subset_def]
   166‚Üí
   167‚Üívariable [SuccOrder M] [NoMaxOrder M]
   168‚Üí
   169‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic multiplicative basis of order `1`
   170‚Üíif it contains an infinite tail of elements. -/
   171‚Üí@[to_additive
   172‚Üí/-- For `M` equipped with a directed order, a set is an asymptotic additive basis of order `1`
   173‚Üíif it contains an infinite tail of consecutive naturals. -/]
   174‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_one_iff_Ici :
   175‚Üí      IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A 1 ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ a, .Ici a ‚äÜ A := by
   176‚Üí  simp [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, cofinite_hasBasis_Ici.eventually_iff, Set.subset_def]
   177‚Üí
   178‚Üí@[to_additive]
   179‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_iff_atTop :
   180‚Üí      IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄŠ∂† a in atTop, a ‚ąą A ^ n := by
   181‚Üí  rw [IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder, cofinite_eq_atTop]
   182‚Üí
   183‚Üí@[to_additive]
   184‚Üílemma isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_iff_prod_atTop :
   185‚Üí    IsAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder A n ‚ÜĒ
   186‚Üí      ‚ąÄŠ∂† a in atTop, ‚ąÉ f : Fin n ‚Üí M, (‚ąÄ i, f i ‚ąą A) ‚ąß ‚ąŹ i, f i = a := by
   187‚Üí  simp [isAsymptoticMulBasisOfOrder_iff_prod, cofinite_eq_atTop]
   188‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 871
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/871](https://www.erdosproblems.com/871)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIf $A$ is an additive basis of order $2$ whose representation function tends to infinity,
    25‚Üícan $A$ always be partitioned into two disjoint additive bases of order $2$?
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[ErNa88] ErdŇĎs, P. and Nathanson, M.B., *Partitions of bases into disjoint unions of bases*,
    28‚ÜíJ. Number Theory (1988).
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[ErNa89] ErdŇĎs, P. and Nathanson, M.B., *Sets of natural numbers with no minimal asymptotic
    31‚Üíbases*, Proc. Amer. Math. Soc. (1989).
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen Classical Filter
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen scoped Topology
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace Erdos871
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- The sumset $A + A = \{a + b : a, b \in A\}$. -/
    41‚Üídef sumset (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    42‚Üí  {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą A, n = a + b}
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an additive basis of order $2$ if every sufficiently large
    45‚Üínatural number can be written as the sum of two elements of $A$. -/
    46‚Üídef IsAdditiveBasis2 (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n ‚Č• N‚āÄ, n ‚ąą sumset A
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- The representation function $r_A(n) = |\{a \in \{0, \ldots, n\} : a \in A \land n - a \in A\}|$,
    50‚Üíi.e., the number of ways to write $n$ as a sum of two elements of $A$. -/
    51‚Üínoncomputable def repCount (A : Set ‚Ąē) (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    52‚Üí  ((Finset.range (n + 1)).filter (fun a => a ‚ąą A ‚ąß (n - a) ‚ąą A)).card
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 871 (DISPROVED) [ErNa88]:
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíLet $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ be an additive basis of order $2$, and suppose
    58‚Üí$1_A * 1_A(n) \to \infty$ as $n \to \infty$ (i.e., the number of representations
    59‚Üíof $n$ as a sum of two elements of $A$ tends to infinity). Can $A$ be partitioned
    60‚Üíinto two disjoint additive bases of order $2$?
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíErdŇĎs and Nathanson proved this is true if $1_A * 1_A(n) > c \log n$ for some
    63‚Üí$c > (\log(4/3))^{-1}$. They also proved [ErNa89] that for every $t$ there exists
    64‚Üía basis $A$ of order $2$ with $1_A * 1_A(n) \geq t$ for all large $n$ that cannot
    65‚Üíbe partitioned into two disjoint additive bases.
    66‚Üí
    67‚ÜíDisproved by Larsen using Claude Opus 4.5 ‚ÄĒ only a small modification of
    68‚Üíthe argument of [ErNa89] is required.
    69‚Üí-/
    70‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    71‚Üítheorem erdos_871 : answer(False) ‚ÜĒ
    72‚Üí    ‚ąÄ A : Set ‚Ąē, IsAdditiveBasis2 A ‚ąß
    73‚Üí      Tendsto (fun n => (repCount A n : ‚ĄĚ)) atTop atTop ‚Üí
    74‚Üí      ‚ąÉ A‚āĀ A‚āā : Set ‚Ąē,
    75‚Üí        A‚āĀ ‚ą™ A‚āā = A ‚ąß Disjoint A‚āĀ A‚āā ‚ąß
    76‚Üí        IsAdditiveBasis2 A‚āĀ ‚ąß IsAdditiveBasis2 A‚āā := by
    77‚Üí  sorry
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üíend Erdos871

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 869
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/869](https://www.erdosproblems.com/869)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA question of ErdŇĎs and Nathanson.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí[ErNa88] ErdŇĎs, P. and Nathanson, M., _Minimal asymptotic bases with prescribed densities_, 1988.
    27‚Üí-/
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üínamespace Erdos869
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí/-- The sumset $A + A$: the set of all $a + b$ with $a, b \in A$. -/
    32‚Üídef sumset (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē := {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą A, n = a + b}
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/-- A set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an additive basis of order $2$ if every sufficiently large
    35‚Üí    natural number can be written as $a + b$ with $a, b \in A$. -/
    36‚Üídef IsAdditiveBasis2 (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    37‚Üí  ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí n ‚ąą sumset A
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- A set $B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is a minimal additive basis of order $2$ if it is an additive
    40‚Üí    basis of order $2$ and for every element $x \in B$, the set of natural numbers
    41‚Üí    not in the sumset of $B \setminus \{x\}$ is infinite. -/
    42‚Üídef IsMinimalAdditiveBasis2 (B : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    43‚Üí  IsAdditiveBasis2 B ‚ąß
    44‚Üí    ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą B, Set.Infinite {n : ‚Ąē | n ‚ąČ sumset (B \ {x})}
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/--
    47‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 869** [ErNa88]:
    48‚Üí
    49‚ÜíIf $A_1$ and $A_2$ are disjoint additive bases of order $2$, must
    50‚Üí$A_1 \cup A_2$ contain a minimal additive basis of order $2$?
    51‚Üí-/
    52‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 11]
    53‚Üítheorem erdos_869 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    54‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (A‚āĀ A‚āā : Set ‚Ąē), IsAdditiveBasis2 A‚āĀ ‚Üí IsAdditiveBasis2 A‚āā ‚Üí Disjoint A‚āĀ A‚āā ‚Üí
    55‚Üí    ‚ąÉ B : Set ‚Ąē, B ‚äÜ A‚āĀ ‚ą™ A‚āā ‚ąß IsMinimalAdditiveBasis2 B := by
    56‚Üí  sorry
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üíend Erdos869

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 818
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/818](https://www.erdosproblems.com/818)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $A$ be a finite set of integers such that $|A+A| \ll |A|$. Is it true that
    25‚Üí$$|AA| \gg \frac{|A|^2}{(\log |A|)^C}$$
    26‚Üífor some constant $C > 0$?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs [Er91]. This was proved by Solymosi [So09d], in the strong form
    29‚Üí$|AA| \gg |A|^2 / \log |A|$.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[Er91] ErdŇĎs, P., _Some of my favourite problems in number theory, combinatorics, and geometry_. Resenhas do Instituto de Matem√°tica e Estat√≠stica da Universidade de S√£o Paulo (1991).
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[So09d] Solymosi, J., _Bounding multiplicative energy by the sumset_. Advances in Mathematics (2009).
    34‚Üí-/
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen Finset Real
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üíopen scoped Pointwise
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üínamespace Erdos818
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/--
    43‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 818** (proved by Solymosi [So09d]):
    44‚Üí
    45‚ÜíThere exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for every $K > 0$, there exists $c > 0$
    46‚Üísuch that for every finite set $A$ of integers with $|A + A| \leq K \cdot |A|$,
    47‚Üíwe have $|A \cdot A| \geq c \cdot |A|^2 / (\log |A|)^C$.
    48‚Üí-/
    49‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    50‚Üítheorem erdos_818 :
    51‚Üí    answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    52‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß
    53‚Üí    ‚ąÄ K : ‚ĄĚ, K > 0 ‚Üí
    54‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß
    55‚Üí    ‚ąÄ A : Finset ‚Ą§, (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• 2 ‚Üí
    56‚Üí    ((A + A).card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ K * A.card ‚Üí
    57‚Üí    ((A * A).card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• c * (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 / (Real.log (A.card : ‚ĄĚ)) ^ C := by
    58‚Üí  sorry
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üíend Erdos818

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 808
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/808](https://www.erdosproblems.com/808)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíErdŇĎs conjectured that for any set $A \subset \mathbb{N}$ with $|A| = n$ and any graph $G$ on $A$
    25‚Üíwith at least $n^{1+c}$ edges, the graph-restricted sumset or product set must satisfy
    26‚Üí$\max(|A +_G A|, |A \cdot_G A|) \geq n^{1+c-\varepsilon}$. Disproved by Alon, Ruzsa, and
    27‚ÜíSolymosi [ARS20].
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[Er77c], [ErSz83], [Er91], [Er97] are references for the original problem.
    30‚Üí-/
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üíopen SimpleGraph Finset
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üínamespace Erdos808
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/-- The graph-restricted sumset $\{f(i) + f(j) : (i, j) \in E(G)\}$. -/
    37‚Üídef graphSumset {n : ‚Ąē} (f : Fin n ‚Üí ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n))
    38‚Üí    [DecidableRel G.Adj] : Finset ‚Ąē :=
    39‚Üí  (Finset.univ.filter (fun p : Fin n √ó Fin n => G.Adj p.1 p.2)).image
    40‚Üí    (fun p => f p.1 + f p.2)
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí/-- The graph-restricted product set $\{f(i) \cdot f(j) : (i, j) \in E(G)\}$. -/
    43‚Üídef graphProdset {n : ‚Ąē} (f : Fin n ‚Üí ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n))
    44‚Üí    [DecidableRel G.Adj] : Finset ‚Ąē :=
    45‚Üí  (Finset.univ.filter (fun p : Fin n √ó Fin n => G.Adj p.1 p.2)).image
    46‚Üí    (fun p => f p.1 * f p.2)
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/--
    49‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 808 (disproved) [Er77c], [ErSz83], [Er91], [Er97]:
    50‚Üí
    51‚ÜíFor all $c, \varepsilon > 0$, for sufficiently large $n$, if $A \subset \mathbb{N}$ has
    52‚Üí$|A| = n$ and $G$ is any graph on $A$ with at least $n^{1+c}$ edges then
    53‚Üí$$
    54‚Üí\max(|A +_G A|, |A \cdot_G A|) \geq n^{1+c-\varepsilon}.
    55‚Üí$$
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíDisproved by Alon, Ruzsa, and Solymosi [ARS20].
    58‚Üí-/
    59‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    60‚Üítheorem erdos_808 : ¬¨
    61‚Üí    (‚ąÄ (c őĶ : ‚ĄĚ), 0 < c ‚Üí 0 < őĶ ‚Üí
    62‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ n ‚Üí
    63‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (f : Fin n ‚Üí ‚Ąē), Function.Injective f ‚Üí
    64‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) [DecidableRel G.Adj],
    65‚Üí    (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ (1 + c) ‚Č§ (G.edgeFinset.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Üí
    66‚Üí    (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ (1 + c - őĶ) ‚Č§
    67‚Üí      max ((graphSumset f G).card : ‚ĄĚ) ((graphProdset f G).card : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
    68‚Üí  sorry
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üíend Erdos808

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 476
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíFor a prime $p$ and $A \subseteq \mathbb{F}_p$, the restricted sumset
    23‚Üí$A \hat{+} A = \{a + b : a, b \in A, a \neq b\}$ satisfies
    24‚Üí$|A \hat{+} A| \geq \min(2|A| - 3, p)$.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/476](https://www.erdosproblems.com/476)
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[dSHa94] da Silva, J.A.D. and Hamidoune, Y.O., *Cyclic spaces for Grassmann derivatives and
    29‚Üíadditive theory*, Bull. London Math. Soc. 26 (1994), 140-146.
    30‚Üí-/
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üíopen Finset
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üínamespace Erdos476
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/--
    37‚ÜíThe restricted sumset $A \hat{+} A = \{a + b : a, b \in A, a \neq b\}$, consisting of all
    38‚Üípairwise sums of distinct elements from $A$.
    39‚Üí-/
    40‚Üídef restrictedSumset {p : ‚Ąē} (A : Finset (ZMod p)) : Finset (ZMod p) :=
    41‚Üí  A.biUnion (fun a => (A.erase a).image (fun b => a + b))

    44‚ÜíErdŇĎs-Heilbronn Conjecture (Problem 476):
    45‚Üí
    46‚ÜíLet $p$ be a prime and let $A \subseteq \mathbb{F}_p$. Define the restricted sumset
    47‚Üí$$A \hat{+} A = \{a + b : a, b \in A, a \neq b\}.$$
    48‚ÜíIs it true that $|A \hat{+} A| \geq \min(2|A| - 3, p)$?
    49‚Üí
    50‚ÜíA question of ErdŇĎs and Heilbronn. Solved in the affirmative by
    51‚Üída Silva and Hamidoune [dSHa94].
    52‚Üí-/
    53‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    54‚Üítheorem erdos_476 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    55‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (p : ‚Ąē) [Fact (Nat.Prime p)] (A : Finset (ZMod p)),
    56‚Üí      (restrictedSumset A).card ‚Č• min (2 * A.card - 3) p := by

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 438
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/438](https://www.erdosproblems.com/438)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíHow large can $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$ be if $A + A$ contains no square numbers?
    25‚Üí
    26‚ÜíTaking all integers $\equiv 1 \pmod{3}$ shows that $|A| \geq N/3$ is possible. This can
    27‚Üíbe improved to $(11/32)N$ by taking all integers $\equiv 1,5,9,13,14,17,21,25,26,29,30
    28‚Üí\pmod{32}$, as observed by Massias.
    29‚Üí
    30‚ÜíKhalfalah, Lodha, and Szemer√©di [KLS02] proved that the maximal such $A$ satisfies
    31‚Üí$|A| \leq (11/32 + o(1))N$, showing that $11/32$ is sharp.
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[KLS02] Khalfalah, A., Lodha, S., and Szemer√©di, E., _On the density of sumsets not
    34‚Üícontaining a square_, 2002.
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üíopen Finset
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üínamespace Erdos438
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/-- No sum of two elements of $A$ is a perfect square. -/
    42‚Üídef SumsetAvoidSquares (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    43‚Üí  ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą A, ¬¨IsSquare (a + b)
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- ErdŇĎs Problem 438 (solved):
    46‚ÜíFor any $\varepsilon > 0$, for all sufficiently large $N$, if $A \subseteq \{1, \ldots, N\}$
    47‚Üíand no sum $a + b$ with $a, b \in A$ is a perfect square, then
    48‚Üí$|A| \leq (11/32 + \varepsilon) \cdot N$.
    49‚Üí
    50‚ÜíProved by Khalfalah, Lodha, and Szemer√©di [KLS02]. -/
    51‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    52‚Üítheorem erdos_438 (őĶ : ‚ĄĚ) (hőĶ : 0 < őĶ) :
    53‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ N ‚Üí
    54‚Üí    ‚ąÄ A : Finset ‚Ąē, (‚ąÄ x ‚ąą A, 1 ‚Č§ x ‚ąß x ‚Č§ N) ‚Üí
    55‚Üí    SumsetAvoidSquares A ‚Üí
    56‚Üí    (A.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (11 / 32 + őĶ) * (N : ‚ĄĚ) := by

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 431
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/431](https://www.erdosproblems.com/431)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíAre there two infinite sets $A$ and $B$ of natural numbers such that the sumset
    25‚Üí$A + B = \{a + b \mid a \in A, b \in B\}$ agrees with the set of prime numbers up to
    26‚Üífinitely many exceptions?
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíThis is a problem of Ostmann, sometimes known as the 'inverse Goldbach problem'.
    29‚ÜíThe expected answer is no.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíElsholtz and Harper [ElHa15] showed that if $A$, $B$ are such sets then for all
    32‚Üílarge $x$ we must have
    33‚Üí$x^{1/2}/(\log x \cdot \log \log x) \ll |A \cap [1,x]| \ll x^{1/2} \cdot \log \log x$
    34‚Üíand similarly for $B$.
    35‚Üí
    36‚ÜíElsholtz [El01] proved there are no sets $A$, $B$, $C$ (all of size at least 2) such that
    37‚Üí$A + B + C$ agrees with the primes up to finitely many exceptions.
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí[ErGr80] ErdŇĎs, P. and Graham, R., _Old and new problems and results in combinatorial
    40‚Üínumber theory_. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí[ElHa15] Elsholtz, C. and Harper, A. J., _Additive decompositions of sets with restricted
    43‚Üíprime factors_, (2015).
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí[El01] Elsholtz, C., _A remark on Hofmann's conjecture_, (2001).
    46‚Üí-/
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üínamespace Erdos431
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/--
    51‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 431 [ErGr80]: Are there two infinite sets $A$ and $B$ of natural
    52‚Üínumbers such that the sumset $A + B = \{a + b \mid a \in A, b \in B\}$ agrees with
    53‚Üíthe set of prime numbers up to finitely many exceptions?
    54‚Üí-/
    55‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 11]
    56‚Üítheorem erdos_431 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    57‚Üí    ‚ąÉ A B : Set ‚Ąē, A.Infinite ‚ąß B.Infinite ‚ąß
    58‚Üí      (symmDiff (Set.image2 (¬∑ + ¬∑) A B) {n | n.Prime}).Finite := by
    59‚Üí  sorry
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üíend Erdos431
    62‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 432
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/432](https://www.erdosproblems.com/432)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíAsked by Straus, inspired by a problem of Ostmann (see Problem #431).
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí[ErGr80] ErdŇĎs, P. and Graham, R., *Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number
    27‚Üítheory*. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen scoped Classical
    31‚Üíopen Filter
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üínamespace Erdos432
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/--
    36‚ÜíThe upper density of $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$:
    37‚Üí$$d^*(A) = \limsup_{N \to \infty} \frac{|A \cap \{0, 1, \ldots, N-1\}|}{N}$$
    38‚Üí-/
    39‚Üínoncomputable def upperDensity (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    40‚Üí  limsup (fun N : ‚Ąē => ((Finset.range N).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card / (N : ‚ĄĚ))
    41‚Üí    atTop
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/--
    44‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 432 [ErGr80, p.85]:
    45‚Üí
    46‚ÜíLet $A, B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ be two infinite sets. If all elements of the sumset
    47‚Üí$A + B = \{a + b \mid a \in A, b \in B\}$ are pairwise coprime, then $A + B$ has
    48‚Üízero upper density.
    49‚Üí-/
    50‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    51‚Üítheorem erdos_432
    52‚Üí    (A B : Set ‚Ąē) (hA : A.Infinite) (hB : B.Infinite)
    53‚Üí    (h_coprime : ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą Set.image2 (¬∑ + ¬∑) A B,
    54‚Üí      ‚ąÄ y ‚ąą Set.image2 (¬∑ + ¬∑) A B, x ‚Č† y ‚Üí Nat.Coprime x y) :
    55‚Üí    upperDensity (Set.image2 (¬∑ + ¬∑) A B) = 0 := by
    56‚Üí  sorry
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üíend Erdos432

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 343
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíIf a multiset of positive integers has positive lower density, must it be subcomplete?
    23‚ÜíThat is, must its set of finite subset sums contain an infinite arithmetic progression?
    24‚ÜíProved by Szemer√©di and Vu.
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/343](https://www.erdosproblems.com/343)
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[ErGr80] ErdŇĎs, P. and Graham, R., *Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number
    29‚Üítheory*. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[SzVu06] Szemer√©di, E. and Vu, V.H., *Finite and infinite arithmetic progressions in sumsets*.
    32‚ÜíAnnals of Mathematics (2006).
    33‚Üí-/
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üíopen scoped BigOperators
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos343
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- The counting function for a multiset represented by multiplicities:
    40‚Üínumber of elements (with multiplicity) in $\{1, \ldots, N\}$. -/
    41‚Üídef countUpTo (mult : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē) (N : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    42‚Üí  ‚ąĎ k ‚ąą Finset.range N, mult (k + 1)
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- The set of all finite subset sums $P(A)$ of a multiset.
    45‚ÜíA finite submultiset is $b : \mathbb{N} \to_0 \mathbb{N}$ with $b(n) \leq \mathrm{mult}(n)$
    46‚Üífor all $n$. The sum is $\sum n \cdot b(n)$. -/
    47‚Üídef subsetSums (mult : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    48‚Üí  {s : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ (b : ‚Ąē ‚Üí‚āÄ ‚Ąē), (‚ąÄ n, b n ‚Č§ mult n) ‚ąß s = b.sum (fun n k => n * k)}
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/-- A set of natural numbers contains an infinite arithmetic progression
    51‚Üí$\{a + nd : n \in \mathbb{N}\}$ for some $a \geq 0$ and $d > 0$. -/
    52‚Üídef ContainsInfiniteAP (S : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    53‚Üí  ‚ąÉ (a d : ‚Ąē), 0 < d ‚ąß ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, a + n * d ‚ąą S
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 343 [ErGr80, p.54]:
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíIf $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is a multiset of integers such that
    59‚Üí$|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}| \gg N$ for all $N$, must $A$ be subcomplete?
    60‚ÜíThat is, must $P(A) = \{\sum_{n \in B} n : B \subseteq A \text{ finite}\}$
    61‚Üícontain an infinite arithmetic progression?
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíA problem of Folkman. Proved by Szemer√©di and Vu [SzVu06].
    64‚Üí-/
    65‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    66‚Üítheorem erdos_343 :
    67‚Üí    answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    68‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (mult : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē),
    69‚Üí        mult 0 = 0 ‚Üí
    70‚Üí        (‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < c ‚ąß ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, 0 < N ‚Üí c * (N : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (countUpTo mult N : ‚ĄĚ)) ‚Üí
    71‚Üí        ContainsInfiniteAP (subsetSums mult) := by
    72‚Üí  sorry
    73‚Üí
    74‚Üíend Erdos343
    75‚Üí

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 344
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/344](https://www.erdosproblems.com/344)
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí[ErGr80] ErdŇĎs, P. and Graham, R., *Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number
    25‚Üítheory*. Monographies de L'Enseignement Math√©matique (1980).
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[SzVu06] Szemer√©di, E. and Vu, V.H., *Finite and infinite arithmetic progressions in sumsets*.
    28‚ÜíAnnals of Mathematics (2006).
    29‚Üí-/
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üíopen Classical
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üínamespace Erdos344
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/-- The subset sum set $P(A)$: the set of all sums $\sum_{n \in B} n$ for finite
    36‚Üí$B \subseteq A$. -/
    37‚Üídef subsetSums (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    38‚Üí  {s : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ (B : Finset ‚Ąē), (‚ÜĎB : Set ‚Ąē) ‚äÜ A ‚ąß B.sum id = s}
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- A set of natural numbers contains an infinite arithmetic progression:
    41‚Üíthere exist $a \geq 0$ and $d > 0$ such that $a + nd \in S$ for all $n \in \mathbb{N}$. -/
    42‚Üídef ContainsInfiniteAP (S : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    43‚Üí  ‚ąÉ (a d : ‚Ąē), 0 < d ‚ąß ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, a + n * d ‚ąą S
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/--
    46‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 344 [ErGr80, p.54]:
    47‚Üí
    48‚ÜíIf $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is a set of positive integers such that
    49‚Üí$|A \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}| \gg N^{1/2}$ (i.e., there exists $c > 0$ and $N_0$ such that
    50‚Üí$|A \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}| \geq c\sqrt{N}$ for all $N \geq N_0$), then $A$ is subcomplete: the set
    51‚Üí$P(A)$ of all finite subset sums of $A$ contains an infinite arithmetic progression.
    52‚Üí
    53‚ÜíThis was proved by Szemer√©di and Vu [SzVu06]. Folkman had previously proved this under the
    54‚Üístronger assumption $|A \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}| \geq cN^{1/2+\varepsilon}$ for some
    55‚Üí$\varepsilon > 0$.
    56‚Üí-/
    57‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    58‚Üítheorem erdos_344
    59‚Üí    (A : Set ‚Ąē)
    60‚Üí    (hA_pos : ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, 0 < a)
    61‚Üí    (c : ‚ĄĚ) (hc : 0 < c)
    62‚Üí    (hgrowth : ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ N ‚Üí
    63‚Üí      c * Real.sqrt (N : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ (((Finset.Icc 1 N).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card : ‚ĄĚ)) :
    64‚Üí    ContainsInfiniteAP (subsetSums A) := by
    65‚Üí  sorry
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üíend Erdos344
    68‚Üí

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 35
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/35](https://www.erdosproblems.com/35)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIf $B$ is an additive basis of order $k$ and $A$ has Schnirelmann density $\alpha$, then
    25‚Üí$d_s(A + B) \geq \alpha + \alpha(1-\alpha)/k$. Proved by Pl√ľnnecke [Pl70].
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Pl70] Pl√ľnnecke, H., _Eine zahlentheoretische Anwendung der Graphentheorie_. J. Reine Angew.
    28‚ÜíMath. 243 (1970), 171‚Äď183.
    29‚Üí-/
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üíopen Classical Finset BigOperators
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üínamespace Erdos35
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí/-- The sumset $A + B$: the set of all $a + b$ with $a \in A$, $b \in B$. -/
    36‚Üídef sumset (A B : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē := {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą B, n = a + b}
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- Schnirelmann density of a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$:
    39‚Üí$$d_s(A) = \inf_{N \geq 1} \frac{|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}|}{N}$$ -/
    40‚Üínoncomputable def schnirelmannDensity (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    41‚Üí  sInf {x : ‚ĄĚ | ‚ąÉ N : ‚Ąē, N ‚Č• 1 ‚ąß x = ((Icc 1 N).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card / (N : ‚ĄĚ)}
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- A set $B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an additive basis of order $k$ if every natural number
    44‚Üícan be written as a sum of exactly $k$ elements from $B$ (with repetition).
    45‚ÜíSince $0 \in B$ is assumed separately, "exactly $k$" is equivalent to "at most $k$". -/
    46‚Üídef IsAdditiveBasis (B : Set ‚Ąē) (k : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    47‚Üí  ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ f : Fin k ‚Üí ‚Ąē, (‚ąÄ i, f i ‚ąą B) ‚ąß ‚ąĎ i, f i = n
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/--
    50‚ÜíLet $B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ be an additive basis of order $k$ with $0 \in B$, and let
    51‚Üí$\alpha = d_s(A)$ be the Schnirelmann density of $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$. Then
    52‚Üí$$d_s(A + B) \geq \alpha + \frac{\alpha(1 - \alpha)}{k}.$$
    53‚Üí
    54‚ÜíThis was proved by Pl√ľnnecke [Pl70], who showed the stronger inequality
    55‚Üí$d_s(A + B) \geq \alpha^{1-1/k}$, as observed by Ruzsa.
    56‚Üí-/
    57‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    58‚Üítheorem erdos_35
    59‚Üí    (A B : Set ‚Ąē) (k : ‚Ąē) (hk : k ‚Č• 1)
    60‚Üí    (hB : IsAdditiveBasis B k) (h0 : (0 : ‚Ąē) ‚ąą B) :
    61‚Üí    let őĪ := schnirelmannDensity A
    62‚Üí    schnirelmannDensity (sumset A B) ‚Č• őĪ + őĪ * (1 - őĪ) / (k : ‚ĄĚ) := by
    63‚Üí  sorry
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üíend Erdos35
    66‚Üí

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 37
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/37](https://www.erdosproblems.com/37)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA lacunary set cannot be an essential component (a set that strictly increases the
    25‚ÜíSchnirelmann density of every set it is added to). Proved by Ruzsa [Ru87].
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[Ru87] Ruzsa, I.Z., _Essential components_. Proc. London Math. Soc. (3) **54** (1987), 38‚Äď56.
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen Classical
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üínamespace Erdos37
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/--
    35‚ÜíThe Schnirelmann density of a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$, defined as
    36‚Üí$$d_s(A) = \inf_{n \geq 1} \frac{|A \cap \{1,\ldots,n\}|}{n}$$
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üínoncomputable def schnirelmannDensity (A : Set ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    39‚Üí  ‚®Ö n : ‚Ąē+, (((Finset.Icc 1 (n : ‚Ąē)).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card : ‚ĄĚ) / ((n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ)
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/--
    42‚ÜíThe sumset $A + B = \{a + b \mid a \in A, b \in B\}$ for sets of natural numbers.
    43‚Üí-/
    44‚Üídef sumset (A B : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    45‚Üí  {n | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą B, n = a + b}
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/--
    48‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an *essential component* if $d_s(A + B) > d_s(B)$ for every
    49‚Üí$B \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ with $0 < d_s(B) < 1$, where $d_s$ is the Schnirelmann density.
    50‚Üí-/
    51‚Üídef IsEssentialComponent (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    52‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (B : Set ‚Ąē), 0 < schnirelmannDensity B ‚Üí schnirelmannDensity B < 1 ‚Üí
    53‚Üí    schnirelmannDensity (sumset A B) > schnirelmannDensity B
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíA set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is *lacunary* if there exists $q > 1$ such that for any two
    57‚Üíconsecutive elements $a < b$ of $A$ (with no element of $A$ strictly between them),
    58‚Üíwe have $b \geq q \cdot a$.
    59‚Üí-/
    60‚Üídef IsLacunary (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    61‚Üí  ‚ąÉ (q : ‚ĄĚ), q > 1 ‚ąß ‚ąÄ (a b : ‚Ąē), a ‚ąą A ‚Üí b ‚ąą A ‚Üí a < b ‚Üí
    62‚Üí    (‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, ¬¨(a < c ‚ąß c < b)) ‚Üí (b : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• q * (a : ‚ĄĚ)
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üí/--
    65‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 37, proved by Ruzsa [Ru87].
    66‚ÜíA lacunary set cannot be an essential component.
    67‚ÜíRuzsa proved that if $A$ is an essential component then there exists some constant
    68‚Üí$c > 0$ such that $|A \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}| \geq (\log N)^{1+c}$ for all large $N$, which rules
    69‚Üíout lacunary sets (which satisfy $|A \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}| = O(\log N)$).
    70‚Üí-/
    71‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    72‚Üítheorem erdos_37 :
    73‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (A : Set ‚Ąē), IsLacunary A ‚Üí ¬¨IsEssentialComponent A := by
    74‚Üí  sorry
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üíend Erdos37
    77‚Üí

    16‚Üí

    21‚Üíopen Function Set
    22‚Üíopen scoped Pointwise
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üívariable {őĪ : Type*} [AddCommMonoid őĪ]
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí/--
    27‚ÜíA set $A$ is said to be sum-free if the sumset $A + A$ is disjoint from $A$, i.e.
    28‚Üíif the equation $a + b = c$ has no solution with $a, b, c \in A$.
    29‚Üí-/
    30‚Üídef IsSumFree (A : Set őĪ) : Prop := Disjoint (A + A) A
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí/-- A set $A$ of natural numbers is said to have bounded gaps if there exists an integer $p$ such
    33‚Üíthat $A ‚ą© [n, n + 1, ..., n + p]$ is nonempty for all $n$. -/
    34‚Üídef IsSyndetic (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Prop := ‚ąÉ p, ‚ąÄ n, (A ‚ą© .Icc n (n + p)).Nonempty
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí/-- A Sidon set is a set, such that such that all pairwise sums of elements are distinct apart from
    37‚Üícoincidences forced by the commutativity of addition. -/
    38‚Üídef IsSidon (A : Set őĪ) : Prop := ‚ąÄŠĶČ (i‚āĀ ‚ąą A) (j‚āĀ ‚ąą A) (i‚āā ‚ąą A) (j‚āā ‚ąą A),
    39‚Üí  i‚āĀ + i‚āā = j‚āĀ + j‚āā ‚Üí (i‚āĀ = j‚āĀ ‚ąß i‚āā = j‚āā) ‚ą® (i‚āĀ = j‚āā ‚ąß i‚āā = j‚āĀ)
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üínamespace Set
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üílemma IsSidon.avoids_isAPOfLength_three {A : Set ‚Ąē} (hA : IsSidon A)
    44‚Üí    {Y : Set ‚Ąē} (hY : Y.IsAPOfLength 3) :
    45‚Üí    (A ‚ą© Y).ncard ‚Č§ 2 := by
    46‚Üí  simp [IsAPOfLength, IsAPOfLengthWith] at hY
    47‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®hc, ‚ü®a, d, hY‚ü©‚ü© := hY
    48‚Üí  have hY_card : Y.ncard = 3 := by simp [ncard, hc]
    49‚Üí  by_contra! h
    50‚Üí  have hss : Y ‚äÜ A ‚ą© Y := by
    51‚Üí    have hY_fin : Finite Y := finite_of_ncard_ne_zero (by linarith)
    52‚Üí    rw [Set.eq_of_subset_of_ncard_le (Set.inter_subset_right) (by linarith)]
    53‚Üí  have ha : a ‚ąą A := mem_of_mem_inter_left <| hss (hY ‚Ėł ‚ü®0, by norm_num, by simp‚ü©)
    54‚Üí  have ha‚āĀ : a + d ‚ąą A := mem_of_mem_inter_left <| hss (hY ‚Ėł ‚ü®1, by norm_num, by simp‚ü©)
    55‚Üí  have ha‚āā : a + 2 ‚ÄĘ d ‚ąą A := mem_of_mem_inter_left <| hss (hY ‚Ėł ‚ü®2, by norm_num, by simp‚ü©)
    56‚Üí  have := hA _ ha _ ha‚āĀ _ ha‚āā _ ha‚āĀ (by simp; omega)
    57‚Üí  simp at this
    58‚Üí  simp [hY, this.1, setOf_and] at hY_card
    59‚Üí  linarith [ncard_singleton _ ‚Ėł ncard_inter_le_ncard_right {a | ‚ąÉ x, x < 3} {a}]
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üítheorem IsSidon.subset {A B : Set őĪ} (hB : IsSidon B) (hAB : A ‚äÜ B) : IsSidon A :=
    62‚Üí  fun _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ‚Ü¶ hB _ (hAB ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ) _ (hAB ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ) _ (hAB ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ) _ (hAB ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ) ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ
    63‚Üí
    64‚Üítheorem IsSidon.insert {A : Set őĪ} {m : őĪ} [IsRightCancelAdd őĪ] [IsLeftCancelAdd őĪ]
    65‚Üí    (hA : IsSidon A) :
    66‚Üí    IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {m}) ‚ÜĒ (m ‚ąą A ‚ą® ‚ąÄŠĶČ (a ‚ąą A) (b ‚ąą A), m + m ‚Č† a + b ‚ąß ‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, m + a ‚Č† b + c) := by
    67‚Üí  by_cases h_mem : m ‚ąą A
    68‚Üí  ¬∑ exact ‚ü®fun _ ‚Ü¶ .inl h_mem, fun _ ‚Ü¶ by rwa [union_singleton, insert_eq_of_mem h_mem]‚ü©
    69‚Üí  refine ‚ü®fun h ‚Ü¶ .inr fun a ha b hb ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®fun hc ‚Ü¶ ?_, fun c hc h_contr ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©, fun hm ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©
    70‚Üí  ¬∑ exact h m (by simp) a (by simp [ha]) m (by simp) b (by simp [hb]) hc
    71‚Üí      |>.elim (fun _ ‚Ü¶ by simp_all) (fun _ ‚Ü¶ by simp_all)
    72‚Üí  ¬∑ exact h m (by simp) b (by simp [hb]) a (by simp [ha]) c (by simp [hc]) h_contr
    73‚Üí      |>.elim (fun _ ‚Ü¶ by simp_all) (fun _ ‚Ü¶ by simp_all)
    74‚Üí  ¬∑ intro i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ
    75‚Üí    rcases hi‚āĀ with (hi‚āĀ | hi‚āĀ)
    76‚Üí    ¬∑ intro j‚āĀ hj‚āĀ
    77‚Üí      rcases hj‚āĀ with (hj‚āĀ | hj‚āĀ)
    78‚Üí      ¬∑ intro i‚āā hi‚āā
    79‚Üí        rcases hi‚āā with (hi‚āā | hi‚āā)
    80‚Üí        ¬∑ intro j‚āā hj‚āā
    81‚Üí          rcases hj‚āā with (hj‚āā | hj‚āā)
    82‚Üí          ¬∑ exact fun h ‚Ü¶ hA i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ j‚āĀ hj‚āĀ i‚āā hi‚āā j‚āā hj‚āā h
    83‚Üí          ¬∑ simp_all
    84‚Üí            exact fun h ‚Ü¶ by cases (hm j‚āĀ hj‚āĀ i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ).2 i‚āā hi‚āā (add_comm j‚āĀ m ‚Ėł h.symm)
    85‚Üí        ¬∑ simp_all
    86‚Üí          exact fun a ha h ‚Ü¶ by cases (hm i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ j‚āĀ hj‚āĀ).2 a ha (add_comm i‚āĀ m ‚Ėł h)
    87‚Üí      ¬∑ simp_all
    88‚Üí        refine ‚ü®fun b hb h ‚Ü¶ .inr <| by simp_all [add_comm], fun b hb ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®fun h ‚Ü¶ ?_, ?_‚ü©‚ü©
    89‚Üí        ¬∑ cases (hm i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ b hb).1 h.symm
    90‚Üí        ¬∑ exact fun c hc h ‚Ü¶ by cases ((hm c hc i‚āĀ hi‚āĀ).2 b hb) h.symm
    91‚Üí    ¬∑ simp_all
    92‚Üí      exact fun _ _ _ _ _ ‚Ü¶ by simp_all [add_comm]
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí
    95‚Üí/-!
    96‚ÜíMaximal Sidon sets in an interval.
    97‚Üí
    98‚ÜíWe follow the convention that `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A ‚äÜ {1, ‚Ä¶, N}` is Sidon and
    99‚Üíis inclusion-maximal among subsets of `Set.Icc 1 N` with the Sidon property.
   100‚Üí-/
   101‚Üí
   102‚Üí/-- `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A ‚äÜ {1, ‚Ä¶, N}` is Sidon and cannot be extended within
   103‚Üí`{1, ‚Ä¶, N}` while remaining Sidon. -/
   104‚Üídef IsMaximalSidonSetIn (A : Set ‚Ąē) (N : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
   105‚Üí  A ‚äÜ Set.Icc 1 N ‚ąß IsSidon A ‚ąß
   106‚Üí    ‚ąÄ ‚¶Éx : ‚Ąē‚¶Ą, x ‚ąą Set.Icc 1 N ‚Üí x ‚ąČ A ‚Üí ¬¨ IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {x})
   107‚Üí
   108‚Üínamespace IsMaximalSidonSetIn
   109‚Üí
   110‚Üí/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, ‚Ä¶, N}`, then `A ‚äÜ {1, ‚Ä¶, N}`. -/
   111‚Üítheorem subset {A : Set ‚Ąē} {N : ‚Ąē} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) :
   112‚Üí    A ‚äÜ Set.Icc 1 N := hA.1
   113‚Üí
   114‚Üí/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, ‚Ä¶, N}`, then `A` is Sidon. -/
   115‚Üítheorem isSidon {A : Set ‚Ąē} {N : ‚Ąē} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) : IsSidon A := hA.2.1
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí/-- Maximality condition unpacked. -/
   118‚Üítheorem maximal {A : Set ‚Ąē} {N : ‚Ąē} (hA : IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N) {x : ‚Ąē}
   119‚Üí    (hx : x ‚ąą Set.Icc 1 N) (hxA : x ‚ąČ A) : ¬¨ IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {x}) := hA.2.2 hx hxA
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üíend IsMaximalSidonSetIn
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üíend Set
   124‚Üí
   125‚Üínamespace Finset
   126‚Üí
   127‚Üíinstance (A : Finset őĪ) [DecidableEq őĪ] : Decidable (IsSidon (A : Set őĪ)) :=
   128‚Üí  decidable_of_iff (‚ąÄŠĶČ (i‚āĀ ‚ąą A) (j‚āĀ ‚ąą A) (i‚āā ‚ąą A) (j‚āā ‚ąą A), _) <| by rfl
   129‚Üí
   130‚Üí/-- The maximum size of a Sidon set in the supplied `Finset`. -/
   131‚Üídef maxSidonSubsetCard (A : Finset őĪ) [DecidableEq őĪ] : ‚Ąē :=
   132‚Üí  (A.powerset.filter fun B : Finset őĪ ‚Ü¶ IsSidon (B : Set őĪ)).sup Finset.card
   133‚Üí
   134‚Üí/-- If `A` is finite Sidon, then `A ‚ą™ {s}` is also Sidon provided `s ‚Č• A.max + 1`. -/
   135‚Üítheorem IsSidon.insert_ge_max' {A : Finset ‚Ąē} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ‚Ąē)) {s : ‚Ąē}
   136‚Üí    (hs : 2 * A.max' h + 1 ‚Č§ s) :
   137‚Üí    IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {s}) := by
   138‚Üí  have h‚āĀ {a b c : ‚Ąē} (ha : a ‚ąą A) (hb : b ‚ąą A) (hc : c ‚ąą A) :
   139‚Üí        a + b < 2 * A.max' h + 1 + c := by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb]
   140‚Üí  have : s ‚ąČ A := by
   141‚Üí    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ |>.2 fun a ha ‚Ü¶ by
   142‚Üí      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   143‚Üí  exact (IsSidon.insert hA).2 <| by simpa [this] using fun a ha b hb ‚Ü¶
   144‚Üí    ‚ü®by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb], fun c hc ‚Ü¶ by linarith [h‚āĀ hc hb ha]‚ü©
   145‚Üí
   146‚Üítheorem IsSidon.exists_insert {A : Finset ‚Ąē} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ‚Ąē)) :
   147‚Üí    ‚ąÉ m ‚ąČ A, IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {m}) := by
   148‚Üí  refine ‚ü®2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, insert_ge_max' h hA le_rfl‚ü©
   149‚Üí  exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ |>.2 fun a ha ‚Ü¶ by
   150‚Üí    linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   151‚Üí
   152‚Üítheorem IsSidon.exists_insert_ge {A : Finset ‚Ąē} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set ‚Ąē)) (s : ‚Ąē) :
   153‚Üí    ‚ąÉ m ‚Č• s, m ‚ąČ A ‚ąß IsSidon (A ‚ą™ {m}) := by
   154‚Üí  refine ‚ü®if s ‚Č• 2 * A.max' h + 1 then s else 2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, ?_, ?_‚ü©
   155‚Üí  ¬∑ split_ifs <;> linarith
   156‚Üí  ¬∑ split_ifs <;>
   157‚Üí    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ |>.2 fun a ha ‚Ü¶ by
   158‚Üí      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   159‚Üí  ¬∑ split_ifs with hs
   160‚Üí    ¬∑ exact insert_ge_max' h hA hs
   161‚Üí    ¬∑ exact insert_ge_max' h hA le_rfl
   162‚Üí
   163‚Üíend Finset
   164‚Üí

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# VC dimension in a group
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíThis file defines the Vapnik‚ÄďChervonenkis (aka VC) dimension of a set in a group, defined as the
    23‚ÜíVC dimension of its family of translates.
    24‚Üí
    25‚ÜíIt also defines the VC‚āô dimension, which has no set family analogue.
    26‚Üí-/
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üíopen scoped Pointwise
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üívariable {G : Type*} [CommGroup G] {A B : Set G} {n d d' : ‚Ąē}
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üívariable (A d) in
    33‚Üí/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    34‚Üí`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]` respectively such that
    35‚Üí`y s * x i ‚ąą A ‚ÜĒ i ‚ąą s` for all `i ‚ąą [d + 1]`, `s ‚äÜ [d + 1]`. -/
    36‚Üí@[to_additive
    37‚Üí/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    38‚Üí`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]` respectively such that
    39‚Üí`y s + x i ‚ąą A ‚ÜĒ i ‚ąą s` for all `i ‚ąą [d + 1]`, `s ‚äÜ [d + 1]`. -/]
    40‚Üídef HasMulVCDimAtMost : Prop :=
    41‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (x : Fin (d + 1) ‚Üí G) (y : Set (Fin (d + 1)) ‚Üí G), ¬¨ ‚ąÄ i s, y s * x i ‚ąą A ‚ÜĒ i ‚ąą s
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí@[to_additive]
    44‚Üílemma HasMulVCDimAtMost.mono (h : d ‚Č§ d') (hd : HasMulVCDimAtMost A d) :
    45‚Üí    HasMulVCDimAtMost A d' := by
    46‚Üí  rintro x y hxy
    47‚Üí  replace h : d + 1 ‚Č§ d' + 1 := by omega
    48‚Üí  exact hd (x ‚ąė Fin.castLE h) (y ‚ąė Set.image (Fin.castLE h)) fun i s ‚Ü¶ (hxy ..).trans <| by simp
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    51‚Üílemma hasMulVCDimAtMost_compl : HasMulVCDimAtMost AŠ∂ú d ‚ÜĒ HasMulVCDimAtMost A d :=
    52‚Üí  forall_congr' fun x ‚Ü¶ (compl_involutive.toPerm.arrowCongr <| .refl _).forall_congr fun y ‚Ü¶
    53‚Üí    not_congr <| forall_congr' fun i ‚Ü¶ compl_involutive.toPerm.forall_congr <| by simp; tauto
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí@[to_additive]
    56‚Üíprotected alias ‚ü®HasMulVCDimAtMost.of_compl, HasMulVCDimAtMost.compl‚ü© := hasMulVCDimAtMost_compl
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    59‚Üíprotected lemma HasMulVCDimAtMost.empty : HasMulVCDimAtMost (‚ąÖ : Set G) d := by
    60‚Üí  simpa [HasMulVCDimAtMost] using ‚ü®default, .univ, by simp‚ü©
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    63‚Üíprotected lemma HasMulVCDimAtMost.univ : HasMulVCDimAtMost (.univ : Set G) d := by
    64‚Üí  simpa [HasMulVCDimAtMost] using ‚ü®default, ‚ąÖ, by simp‚ü©
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí@[to_additive (attr := simp)]
    67‚Üílemma hasVCDimAtMost_smul : HasVCDimAtMost {t ‚ÄĘ A | t : G} d ‚ÜĒ HasMulVCDimAtMost A d := by
    68‚Üí  simpa [HasVCDimAtMost, HasMulVCDimAtMost, Classical.skolem (b := fun _ ‚Ü¶ G), ‚Üź funext_iff,
    69‚Üí    Set.mem_smul_set_iff_inv_smul_mem]
    70‚Üí    using forall_congr' fun x ‚Ü¶ (Equiv.inv _).forall_congr <| by simp
    71‚Üí
    72‚Üívariable (A d n) in
    73‚Üí/-- A set `A` in an abelian group has VC‚āô dimension at most `d` iff one cannot find two sequences
    74‚Üí`x` and `y` of elements indexed by `[n] √ó [d + 1]` and `2 ^ [d + 1]‚ĀŅ` respectively such that
    75‚Üí`y s * ‚ąŹ k, x (k, i k) ‚ąą A ‚ÜĒ i ‚ąą s` for all `i ‚ąą [d + 1]‚ĀŅ`, `s ‚äÜ [d + 1]‚ĀŅ`. -/

    41‚Üí/-- $n$ is the sum of at most $t$ distinct elements from $A$:

    61‚Üí    ‚ąÄŠ∂† n : ‚Ąē in atTop, ‚ąÄ A : Finset ‚Ą§, A.card = n ‚Üí
    62‚Üí      (maxRestrictedSumAvoidingSubsetSize A : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• (Real.log n) ^ 100 := by

    74‚Üí/--

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 154
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/154](https://www.erdosproblems.com/154)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $A \subset \{1, \ldots, N\}$ be a Sidon set with $|A| \sim N^{1/2}$.
    25‚ÜíMust the sumset $A + A$ be well-distributed over all small moduli?
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[ESS94] ErdŇĎs, P., S√°rk√∂zy, A., and S√≥s, V. T., 1994.
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üí[Li98] Lindstr√∂m, B., 1998.
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[Ko99] Kolountzakis, M., 1999.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen Filter
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üíopen scoped Topology Real
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üínamespace Erdos154
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí/-- A finite set of natural numbers is a Sidon set (also called a $B_2$ set) if all
    41‚Üípairwise sums $a + b$ (allowing $a = b$) are distinct: whenever $a + b = c + d$
    42‚Üíwith $a, b, c, d \in A$, we have $\{a, b\} = \{c, d\}$ as multisets. Equivalently,
    43‚Üíall differences $a - b$ with $a \neq b$ and $a, b \in A$ are distinct. -/
    44‚Üídef IsSidonSet (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    45‚Üí  ‚ąÄ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ c ‚ąą A, ‚ąÄ d ‚ąą A,
    46‚Üí    a + b = c + d ‚Üí (a = c ‚ąß b = d) ‚ą® (a = d ‚ąß b = c)
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí/-- The sumset $A + A = \{a + b \mid a, b \in A\}$. -/
    49‚Üídef sumset (A : Finset ‚Ąē) : Finset ‚Ąē := Finset.image‚āā (¬∑ + ¬∑) A A
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- The fraction of elements in a finite set of naturals that are congruent to $r$ modulo $m$. -/
    52‚Üínoncomputable def modFraction (m r : ‚Ąē) (S : Finset ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    53‚Üí  ((S.filter (fun n => n % m = r)).card : ‚ĄĚ) / (S.card : ‚ĄĚ)
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/--
    56‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem \#154 [ESS94]:
    57‚Üí
    58‚ÜíLet $A \subset \{1, \ldots, N\}$ be a Sidon set with $|A| \sim N^{1/2}$. Must $A + A$ be
    59‚Üíwell-distributed over all small moduli? In particular, must about half
    60‚Üíthe elements of $A + A$ be even and half odd?
    61‚Üí
    62‚ÜíProved in the affirmative. Lindstr√∂m [Li98] showed that $A$ itself is
    63‚Üíwell-distributed modulo small integers (e.g. $|A \cap \{\text{evens}\}| \approx |A|/2$),
    64‚Üísubsequently strengthened by Kolountzakis [Ko99]. The extension to $A + A$
    65‚Üífollows immediately from the Sidon property: if $A$ has $e$ even and $o$ odd
    66‚Üíelements, then $A + A$ has exactly $e(e+1)/2 + o(o+1)/2$ even elements
    67‚Üíand $eo$ odd elements (all distinct by the Sidon property), and the
    68‚Üídistribution is approximately $1/2$ each when $e \approx o \approx |A|/2$.
    69‚Üí
    70‚ÜíFormalized as: for any sequence $(A_n)_n$ of Sidon sets $A_n \subset \{0, \ldots, n\}$
    71‚Üíwith $|A_n| / \sqrt{n} \to 1$ as $n \to \infty$, and any fixed modulus $m \geq 1$ and
    72‚Üíresidue $0 \leq r < m$, the fraction of elements of $A_n + A_n$ in residue
    73‚Üíclass $r \bmod m$ tends to $1/m$.
    74‚Üí-/
    75‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    76‚Üítheorem erdos_154 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    77‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (A : ‚Ąē ‚Üí Finset ‚Ąē),
    78‚Üí      (‚ąÄ n, IsSidonSet (A n)) ‚Üí
    79‚Üí      (‚ąÄ n, (A n) ‚äÜ Finset.range (n + 1)) ‚Üí
    80‚Üí      Tendsto (fun n => ((A n).card : ‚ĄĚ) / Real.sqrt n) atTop (ūĚďĚ 1) ‚Üí
    81‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (m : ‚Ąē), 1 ‚Č§ m ‚Üí
    82‚Üí        ‚ąÄ r < m,
    83‚Üí          Tendsto (fun n => modFraction m r (sumset (A n))) atTop (ūĚďĚ (1 / (m : ‚ĄĚ))) := by
    84‚Üí  sorry
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üíend Erdos154

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 337
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/337](https://www.erdosproblems.com/337)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIs it true that for an additive basis $A$ of density $o(N)$, the ratio
    25‚Üí$|(A+A) \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}| / |A \cap \{1,\ldots,N\}|$ tends to infinity?
    26‚ÜíThe answer is no, as shown by Turj√°nyi.
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[ErGr80] ErdŇĎs, P. and Graham, R., *Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number
    29‚Üítheory*. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí[ErGr80b] ErdŇĎs, P. and Graham, R., *Old and new problems and results in combinatorial number
    32‚Üítheory II*. (1980).
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí[Tu84] Turj√°nyi, S., *On a question of ErdŇĎs and Graham*. (1984).
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[RT85] Ruzsa, I.Z. and Turj√°nyi, S., *A note on additive bases*. (1985).
    37‚Üí-/
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üíopen Filter
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üíopen scoped Topology BigOperators
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üínamespace Erdos337
    44‚Üí
    45‚Üí/-- The sumset $A + A = \{a + b : a, b \in A\}$. -/
    46‚Üídef sumset (A : Set ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    47‚Üí  {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą A, ‚ąÉ b ‚ąą A, n = a + b}
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- The set of all sums of exactly $k$ elements from $A$ (with repetition). -/
    50‚Üídef exactSumset (A : Set ‚Ąē) (k : ‚Ąē) : Set ‚Ąē :=
    51‚Üí  {n : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ (f : Fin k ‚Üí ‚Ąē), (‚ąÄ i, f i ‚ąą A) ‚ąß n = ‚ąĎ i, f i}
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/-- $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an additive basis of order $r$ if every sufficiently large
    54‚Üínatural number is the sum of at most $r$ elements from $A$. -/
    55‚Üídef IsAdditiveBasis (A : Set ‚Ąē) (r : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    56‚Üí  ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n ‚Č• N‚āÄ, ‚ąÉ k ‚Č§ r, n ‚ąą exactSumset A k
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí/-- Count of elements of $A$ in $\{0, 1, \ldots, N\}$. -/
    59‚Üínoncomputable def countInRange (A : Set ‚Ąē) (N : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    60‚Üí  haveI : DecidablePred (¬∑ ‚ąą A) := Classical.decPred _
    61‚Üí  ((Finset.range (N + 1)).filter (¬∑ ‚ąą A)).card
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/--
    64‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 337 [ErGr80] [ErGr80b]:
    65‚Üí
    66‚ÜíLet $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ be an additive basis (of any finite order) such that
    67‚Üí$|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}| = o(N)$. Is it true that
    68‚Üí$$\lim_{N \to \infty} \frac{|(A+A) \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}|}{|A \cap \{1, \ldots, N\}|} = \infty?$$
    69‚Üí
    70‚ÜíThe answer is no. A counterexample was provided by Turj√°nyi [Tu84].
    71‚ÜíThis was generalised (replacing $A+A$ by the $h$-fold sumset $hA$ for any
    72‚Üí$h \geq 2$) by Ruzsa and Turj√°nyi [RT85].
    73‚Üí-/
    74‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    75‚Üítheorem erdos_337 : answer(False) ‚ÜĒ
    76‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (A : Set ‚Ąē),
    77‚Üí      (‚ąÉ r : ‚Ąē, IsAdditiveBasis A r) ‚Üí
    78‚Üí      Tendsto (fun N => (countInRange A N : ‚ĄĚ) / (N : ‚ĄĚ)) atTop (nhds 0) ‚Üí
    79‚Üí      Tendsto (fun N => (countInRange (sumset A) N : ‚ĄĚ) /
    80‚Üí          (countInRange A N : ‚ĄĚ)) atTop atTop := by
    81‚Üí  sorry
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üíend Erdos337

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 449
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/449](https://www.erdosproblems.com/449)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $r(n)$ count the number of pairs $(d_1, d_2)$ such that $d_1 \mid n$ and
    25‚Üí$d_2 \mid n$ and $d_1 < d_2 < 2d_1$. Is it true that, for every $\epsilon > 0$,
    26‚Üí$r(n) < \epsilon \tau(n)$ for almost all $n$, where $\tau(n)$ is the number of
    27‚Üídivisors of $n$?
    28‚Üí
    29‚ÜíThis is false. For any constant $K > 0$, we have $r(n) > K\tau(n)$ for a
    30‚Üípositive density set of $n$. Kevin Ford observed this follows from the negative
    31‚Üísolution to [448] via the Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz inequality.
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen Classical
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos449
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- $r(n)$: the number of ordered pairs $(d_1, d_2)$ of divisors of $n$ with
    39‚Üí$d_1 < d_2 < 2 \cdot d_1$. -/
    40‚Üídef closeDivisorPairs (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    41‚Üí  ((n.divisors √óňĘ n.divisors).filter (fun p => p.1 < p.2 ‚ąß p.2 < 2 * p.1)).card
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/-- ErdŇĎs Problem 449 (DISPROVED by Ford, via the negative solution to [448]):
    44‚Üí
    45‚ÜíIs it true that for every $\epsilon > 0$, $r(n) < \epsilon \cdot \tau(n)$ for
    46‚Üíalmost all $n$? The answer is no. -/
    47‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    48‚Üítheorem erdos_449 : answer(False) ‚ÜĒ
    49‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, őĶ > 0 ‚Üí
    50‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őī : ‚ĄĚ, őī > 0 ‚Üí
    51‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ N ‚Üí
    52‚Üí      ((Finset.Icc 1 N).filter (fun n =>
    53‚Üí        (closeDivisorPairs n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• őĶ * (n.divisors.card : ‚ĄĚ))).card /
    54‚Üí          (N : ‚ĄĚ) < őī := by
    55‚Üí  sorry
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üíend Erdos449

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 281
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/281](https://www.erdosproblems.com/281)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $n_1 < n_2 < \cdots$ be a strictly increasing sequence of positive integers such that,
    25‚Üífor any choice of congruence classes $a_i \pmod{n_i}$, the integers not satisfying any of the
    26‚Üícongruences have density $0$. Is it true that for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists some $k$
    27‚Üísuch that the density of integers avoiding the first $k$ congruences is already less than
    28‚Üí$\varepsilon$, regardless of the choice of $a_i$?
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[ErGr80] ErdŇĎs, P. and Graham, R., _Old and new problems and results in combinatorial
    31‚Üínumber theory_. Monographies de L'Enseignement Mathematique (1980).
    32‚Üí-/
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üíopen Classical
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üínamespace Erdos281
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí/-- Count of integers in $\{0, \ldots, N-1\}$ not in any congruence class $a(i) \bmod n(i)$
    39‚Üífor all $i$. -/
    40‚Üínoncomputable def avoidCountAll (n a : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē) (N : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    41‚Üí  ((Finset.range N).filter fun m =>
    42‚Üí    ‚ąÄ i : ‚Ąē, ¬¨(m ‚Č° a i [MOD n i])).card
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- Count of integers in $\{0, \ldots, N-1\}$ avoiding congruences $a(i) \bmod n(i)$
    45‚Üífor $i < k$. -/
    46‚Üínoncomputable def avoidCountFin (n a : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē) (k N : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    47‚Üí  ((Finset.range N).filter fun m =>
    48‚Üí    ‚ąÄ i, i < k ‚Üí ¬¨(m ‚Č° a i [MOD n i])).card
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí/--
    51‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 281 (Proved) [ErGr80, p.29]:
    52‚Üí
    53‚ÜíLet $n_1 < n_2 < \cdots$ be a strictly increasing sequence of positive integers such that,
    54‚Üífor any choice of congruence classes $a_i \pmod{n_i}$, the set of integers not satisfying
    55‚Üíany of the congruences has density $0$. Then for every $\varepsilon > 0$ there exists some $k$
    56‚Üísuch that, for every choice of congruence classes $a_i$, the density of integers not
    57‚Üísatisfying any of the congruences $a_i \pmod{n_i}$ for $i < k$ is less than $\varepsilon$.
    58‚Üí
    59‚ÜíThe proof combines the Davenport‚ÄďErdŇĎs theorem with Rogers' optimal sieve bound.
    60‚Üí-/
    61‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 11]
    62‚Üítheorem erdos_281 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    63‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē), (‚ąÄ i, 0 < n i) ‚Üí StrictMono n ‚Üí
    64‚Üí    (‚ąÄ a : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí
    65‚Üí      ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ N, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ N ‚Üí (avoidCountAll n a N : ‚ĄĚ) / (N : ‚ĄĚ) < őĶ) ‚Üí
    66‚Üí    ‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí ‚ąÉ k : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ a : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē,
    67‚Üí      ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ N, N‚āÄ ‚Č§ N ‚Üí (avoidCountFin n a k N : ‚ĄĚ) / (N : ‚ĄĚ) < őĶ := by
    68‚Üí  sorry
    69‚Üí
    70‚Üíend Erdos281

    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 106
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/106](https://www.erdosproblems.com/106)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíDraw $n$ non-overlapping squares (possibly rotated) inside the unit square. Let $f(n)$ be
    25‚Üíthe maximum total side length. Is $f(k^2 + 1) = k$ for every positive integer $k$?
    26‚Üí-/
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üíopen BigOperators Real
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üínamespace Erdos106
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí/--
    33‚ÜíA square placement in $\mathbb{R}^2$: a center point, a positive side length, and a
    34‚Üírotation angle (in radians) measuring how far the square is rotated from
    35‚Üíthe standard axis-aligned orientation.
    36‚Üí-/
    37‚Üístructure SquarePlacement where
    38‚Üí  center : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ
    39‚Üí  side   : ‚ĄĚ
    40‚Üí  angle  : ‚ĄĚ
    41‚Üí  side_pos : 0 < side
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí/--
    44‚ÜíThe closed region occupied by a square placement. A point $p$ lies in the
    45‚Üíregion iff, when translated so the center is at the origin and then rotated
    46‚Üíby $-\mathrm{angle}$, its coordinates both lie in $[-\mathrm{side}/2, \mathrm{side}/2]$.
    47‚Üí-/
    48‚Üínoncomputable def SquarePlacement.region (sq : SquarePlacement) : Set (‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ) :=
    49‚Üí  {p : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ |
    50‚Üí    let u :=  (p.1 - sq.center.1) * cos sq.angle + (p.2 - sq.center.2) * sin sq.angle
    51‚Üí    let v := -(p.1 - sq.center.1) * sin sq.angle + (p.2 - sq.center.2) * cos sq.angle
    52‚Üí    |u| ‚Č§ sq.side / 2 ‚ąß |v| ‚Č§ sq.side / 2}
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚ÜíThe open interior of a square placement (strict inequalities).
    56‚Üí-/
    57‚Üínoncomputable def SquarePlacement.sqInterior (sq : SquarePlacement) : Set (‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ) :=
    58‚Üí  {p : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ |
    59‚Üí    let u :=  (p.1 - sq.center.1) * cos sq.angle + (p.2 - sq.center.2) * sin sq.angle
    60‚Üí    let v := -(p.1 - sq.center.1) * sin sq.angle + (p.2 - sq.center.2) * cos sq.angle
    61‚Üí    |u| < sq.side / 2 ‚ąß |v| < sq.side / 2}
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/--
    64‚ÜíThe unit square $[0,1]^2 \subseteq \mathbb{R}^2$.
    65‚Üí-/
    66‚Üídef unitSquare : Set (‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ) :=
    67‚Üí  {p : ‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ | 0 ‚Č§ p.1 ‚ąß p.1 ‚Č§ 1 ‚ąß 0 ‚Č§ p.2 ‚ąß p.2 ‚Č§ 1}
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí/--
    70‚ÜíA valid configuration of $n$ squares inside the unit square:
    71‚Üí1. Each square's closed region is contained in the unit square.
    72‚Üí2. No two distinct squares share an interior point (their open interiors
    73‚Üí   are disjoint).
    74‚Üí-/
    75‚Üídef IsValidSquareConfig (n : ‚Ąē) (config : Fin n ‚Üí SquarePlacement) : Prop :=
    76‚Üí  (‚ąÄ i : Fin n, (config i).region ‚äÜ unitSquare) ‚ąß
    77‚Üí  (‚ąÄ i j : Fin n, i ‚Č† j ‚Üí Disjoint (config i).sqInterior (config j).sqInterior)
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí/--
    80‚Üí$f(n)$ is the supremum of the total side-length sum over all valid configurations
    81‚Üíof $n$ (possibly rotated) squares inside the unit square with pairwise disjoint
    82‚Üíinteriors.
    83‚Üí-/
    84‚Üínoncomputable def f (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚ĄĚ :=
    85‚Üí  sSup {s : ‚ĄĚ | ‚ąÉ config : Fin n ‚Üí SquarePlacement,
    86‚Üí    IsValidSquareConfig n config ‚ąß s = ‚ąĎ i : Fin n, (config i).side}
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí/--
    89‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 106:
    90‚Üí
    91‚ÜíDraw $n$ squares (not necessarily axis-aligned) inside the unit square $[0,1]^2$
    92‚Üíwith no two squares sharing a common interior point. Let $f(n)$ be the maximum
    93‚Üípossible sum of the side-lengths of the squares.
    94‚Üí
    95‚Üí**Conjecture**: $f(k^2 + 1) = k$ for every positive integer $k$.
    96‚Üí
    97‚ÜíBackground:
    98‚Üí- It follows easily from the Cauchy‚ÄďSchwarz inequality that $f(k^2) = k$.
    99‚Üí- The lower bound $f(k^2 + 1) \geq k$ is elementary: subdivide $[0,1]^2$ into $k^2$
   100‚Üí  squares of side $1/k$, then replace any one of them by two squares of side
   101‚Üí  $1/(2k)$; the total side-length is $(k^2 - 1)/k + 2 \cdot 1/(2k) = k$.
   102‚Üí- The conjecture asserts that this lower bound is tight: no configuration of
   103‚Üí  $k^2 + 1$ squares can exceed the total side-length $k$ achievable with $k^2$ squares.
   104‚Üí- Baek, Koizumi, and Ueoro (2024) proved the axis-aligned variant: if all
   105‚Üí  squares are required to have sides parallel to the coordinate axes, then the
   106‚Üí  supremum equals $k$.
   107‚Üí-/
   108‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 52]
   109‚Üítheorem erdos_106 :
   110‚Üí    answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, 0 < k ‚Üí f (k ^ 2 + 1) = (k : ‚ĄĚ) := by
   111‚Üí  sorry
   112‚Üí
   113‚Üíend Erdos106
   114‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Green's Open Problem 85
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Carbery‚Äôs rectangle problem*
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíReferences:
    25‚Üí- [Gr24] [Green, Ben. "100 open problems." (2024).](https://people.maths.ox.ac.uk/greenbj/papers/open-problems.pdf#problem.85)
    26‚Üí- [CCW99] Carbery, Anthony, Michael Christ, and James Wright. "Multidimensional van der Corput and sublevel set estimates." Journal of the American Mathematical Society 12.4 (1999): 981-1015 Section 6.
    27‚Üí- [Ke00] Keleti, Tam√°s. "Density and covering properties of intervals of ‚ĄĚn." Mathematika 47.1-2 (2000): 229-242.
    28‚Üí- [KKM02] Katz, Nets Hawk, Elliot Krop, and Mauro Maggioni. "Remarks on the box problem." Mathematical Research Letters 9.4 (2002): 515-520.
    29‚Üí- [Mu02] Mubayi, Dhruv. "Some exact results and new asymptotics for hypergraph Tur√°n numbers." Combinatorics, Probability and Computing 11.3 (2002): 299-309 Conjecture 1.4.
    30‚Üí- [CPZ20] Conlon, David, Cosmin Pohoata, and Dmitriy Zakharov. "Random multilinear maps and the Erd\H {o} s box problem." arXiv preprint arXiv:2011.09024 (2020).
    31‚Üí-/
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üíopen Filter MeasureTheory Set
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üínamespace Green85
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/--
    38‚ÜíSuppose that $A$ is an open subset of $[0, 1]^2$ with measure $\alpha$. Are there four points in
    39‚Üí$A$ determining an axis-parallel rectangle with area $\gt c \alpha^2$?
    40‚Üí-/
    41‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 28 52]
    42‚Üítheorem green_85 :
    43‚Üí  answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ ‚ąÉ c > 0, ‚ąÄ A : Set (‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ),
    44‚Üí    IsOpen A ‚Üí
    45‚Üí    A ‚äÜ Icc 0 1 √óňĘ Icc 0 1 ‚Üí
    46‚Üí    A.Nonempty ‚Üí
    47‚Üí    let őĪ := (volume A).toReal
    48‚Üí    ‚ąÉ x‚āĀ x‚āā y‚āĀ y‚āā,
    49‚Üí      {(x‚āĀ, y‚āĀ), (x‚āā, y‚āĀ), (x‚āā, y‚āā), (x‚āĀ, y‚āā)} ‚äÜ A ‚ąß
    50‚Üí      c * őĪ ^ 2 ‚Č§ |x‚āĀ - x‚āā| * |y‚āĀ - y‚āā| := by
    51‚Üí  sorry
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí/--
    54‚ÜíFrom [Gr24] "It is quite easy to show using Cauchy-Schwarz that there must be such a rectangle with
    55‚Üíarea $\gg \alpha^2 (\log 1/\alpha)^{-1}$."
    56‚Üí-/
    57‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 28 52]
    58‚Üítheorem green_85_loose :
    59‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c > 0, ‚ąÄ A : Set (‚ĄĚ √ó ‚ĄĚ),
    60‚Üí    IsOpen A ‚Üí
    61‚Üí    A ‚äÜ Icc 0 1 √óňĘ Icc 0 1 ‚Üí
    62‚Üí    A.Nonempty ‚Üí
    63‚Üí    let őĪ := (volume A).toReal
    64‚Üí    ‚ąÉ x‚āĀ x‚āā y‚āĀ y‚āā,
    65‚Üí      {(x‚āĀ, y‚āĀ), (x‚āā, y‚āĀ), (x‚āā, y‚āā), (x‚āĀ, y‚āā)} ‚äÜ A ‚ąß
    66‚Üí      őĪ ^ 2 * (Real.log (1/őĪ))‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚Č§ |x‚āĀ - x‚āā| * |y‚āĀ - y‚āā| := by
    67‚Üí  sorry
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üíend Green85
    70‚Üí

    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíDefine $r_k(d_1, d_2)$ to be the smallest integer $r$ such that for any lacunary sequence $B$

    34‚Üí/-- The $k$-fold sumset of a set $S$ of natural numbers: all sums $s_1 + \cdots + s_k$
    35‚Üíwith each $s_i \in S$. Defined recursively: $0S = \{0\}$,
    36‚Üí$(k+1)S = \{a + b \mid a \in S,\, b \in kS\}$. -/

    43‚Üí
    44‚ÜíDefine $r_k(d_1, d_2)$ to be the smallest integer $r$ (if it exists) such that for any
    45‚Üílacunary sequence $B = \{b_1 < b_2 < \cdots\}$ of positive integers with

    48‚Üí$(kA) \cap B = \emptyset$, where $kA$ is the $k$-fold sumset.

    24‚ÜíLet $g_d(n)$ be minimal such that every collection of $g_d(n)$ points in $\mathbb{R}^d$

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 949
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/949](https://www.erdosproblems.com/949)
    23‚Üí-/
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üíopen Cardinal Filter
    26‚Üíopen scoped Pointwise Topology
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üínamespace Erdos949
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí/--
    32‚ÜíLet $S \subseteq \mathbb{R}$ be a set containing no solutions to $a + b = c$.
    33‚ÜíMust there be a set $A \subseteq \mathbb{R} \setminus S$ of cardinality continuum such that
    34‚Üí$A + A \subseteq \mathbb{R}\setminus S$?
    35‚Üí-/
    36‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    37‚Üítheorem erdos_949 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    38‚Üí    ‚ąÄ S : Set ‚ĄĚ, (‚ąÄ a ‚ąą S, ‚ąÄ b ‚ąą S, a + b ‚ąČ S) ‚Üí ‚ąÉ A ‚äÜ SŠ∂ú, #A = ūĚĒ† ‚ąß A + A ‚äÜ SŠ∂ú := by
    39‚Üí  sorry
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/-- Let $S\sub \mathbb{R}$ be a Sidon set. Must there be a set $A\sub \mathbb{R}‚ąĖS$ of cardinality
    42‚Üícontinuum such that $A + A \sub \mathbb{R}‚ąĖS$? -/
    43‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    44‚Üítheorem erdos_949.variants.sidon : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    45‚Üí    ‚ąÄ S : Set ‚ĄĚ, IsSidon S ‚Üí ‚ąÉ A ‚äÜ SŠ∂ú, #A = ūĚĒ† ‚ąß A + A ‚äÜ SŠ∂ú := by
    46‚Üí  simp only [true_iff, Set.add_subset_iff]
    47‚Üí  rintro S hS
    48‚Üí  -- We case on whether `S` has cardinality the continuum or strictly less.
    49‚Üí  obtain hSūĚĒ† | hSūĚĒ† : #S < ūĚĒ† ‚ą® #S = ūĚĒ† := lt_or_eq_of_le <| by simpa using mk_set_le S
    50‚Üí  -- If `S` has cardinality strictly less than the continuum, then we pick by Zorn `A` maximal
    51‚Üí  -- such that both `A` and `A + A` are disjoint from `S`.
    52‚Üí  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®A, ‚ü®hAS, hAAS‚ü©, hAmax‚ü© := by
    53‚Üí      refine zorn_subset {A ‚äÜ SŠ∂ú | ‚ąÄ x ‚ąą A,‚ąÄ y ‚ąą A, x + y ‚ąČ S} ?_
    54‚Üí      simp only [Set.setOf_and, Set.subset_inter_iff, Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq, and_imp,
    55‚Üí        and_assoc]
    56‚Üí      refine fun C hCS hSC hC ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®_, Set.iUnion‚āā_subset hCS, ?_, Set.subset_iUnion‚āā‚ü©
    57‚Üí      simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, exists_prop, forall_exists_index, and_imp]
    58‚Üí      rintro x A hA hx y B hB hy
    59‚Üí      obtain ‚ü®D, hD, hAD, hBD‚ü© := hC.directedOn _ hA _ hB
    60‚Üí      exact hSC hD _ (hAD hx) _ (hBD hy)
    61‚Üí    -- By construction, `A` satisfies all properties except possibly for having size the continuum.
    62‚Üí    refine ‚ü®A, hAS, ?_, hAAS‚ü©
    63‚Üí    -- By maximality, `SŠ∂ú ‚ą© (S / 2)Š∂ú ‚äÜ A ‚ą™ ‚čÉ a ‚ąą A, (S - a)`.
    64‚Üí    replace hAmax : SŠ∂ú ‚ą© ((¬∑ / 2) '' S)Š∂ú ‚äÜ A ‚ą™ ‚čÉ a ‚ąą A, (¬∑ - a) '' S
    65‚Üí    ¬∑ simp only [Set.subset_def, Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_compl_iff, Set.mem_image, ne_eq,
    66‚Üí      OfNat.ofNat_ne_zero, not_false_eq_true, div_eq_iff_mul_eq, mul_two, exists_eq_right',
    67‚Üí      Set.mem_union, Set.mem_iUnion, sub_eq_iff_eq_add, exists_eq_right, exists_prop,
    68‚Üí      or_iff_not_imp_left, and_imp]
    69‚Üí      rintro x hxS hxxS hxA
    70‚Üí      by_contra! hxAS
    71‚Üí      refine hxA <| hAmax ?_ (Set.subset_insert ..) (Set.mem_insert ..)
    72‚Üí      simpa [Set.insert_subset_iff, forall_and, add_comm _ x, *] using ‚ü®hxAS, hAAS‚ü©
    73‚Üí    -- By assumption, `#(SŠ∂ú ‚ą© (S / 2)Š∂ú) = ūĚĒ†`.
    74‚Üí    have hSūĚĒ†' : #‚ÜĎ(SŠ∂ú ‚ą© ((¬∑ / 2) '' S)Š∂ú) = ūĚĒ†
    75‚Üí    ¬∑ rw [‚Üź Set.compl_union, mk_compl_of_infinite, mk_real]
    76‚Üí      grw [mk_union_le, Cardinal.mk_real]
    77‚Üí      refine add_lt_of_lt aleph0_le_continuum hSūĚĒ† ?_
    78‚Üí      grw [mk_image_le]
    79‚Üí      exact hSūĚĒ†
    80‚Üí    -- If `#A < ūĚĒ†`, we would then have
    81‚Üí    -- `ūĚĒ† = #(SŠ∂ú ‚ą© (S / 2)Š∂ú) ‚Č§ #(A ‚ą™ ‚čÉ a ‚ąą A, (S - a)) ‚Č§ #A + #A * #S < ūĚĒ†`, contradiction.
    82‚Üí    refine (mk_real ‚Ėł mk_set_le _).eq_of_not_lt fun hAūĚĒ† ‚Ü¶ lt_irrefl ūĚĒ† ?_
    83‚Üí    calc
    84‚Üí      ūĚĒ† = #‚ÜĎ(SŠ∂ú ‚ą© ((¬∑ / 2) '' S)Š∂ú) := by rw [hSūĚĒ†']
    85‚Üí      _ ‚Č§ #‚ÜĎ(A ‚ą™ ‚čÉ a ‚ąą A, (¬∑ - a) '' S) := mk_subtype_mono hAmax
    86‚Üí      _ ‚Č§ #A + #A * #S := by
    87‚Üí        obtain rfl | hA := A.eq_empty_or_nonempty
    88‚Üí        ¬∑ simp
    89‚Üí        have : Nonempty A := hA.coe_sort
    90‚Üí        grw [mk_union_le, mk_biUnion_le, ciSup_le fun _ ‚Ü¶ mk_image_le]
    91‚Üí      _ < ūĚĒ† := add_lt_of_lt aleph0_le_continuum hAūĚĒ† <| mul_lt_of_lt aleph0_le_continuum hAūĚĒ† hSūĚĒ†
    92‚Üí  -- If `S` has cardinality the continuum, then we pick some `a ‚Č† 0` in `S` and set
    93‚Üí  -- `A := (S \ {a} - a / 2) \ S`.
    94‚Üí  have hSinf : S.Infinite := by simpa using aleph0_le_continuum.trans_eq hSūĚĒ†.symm
    95‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®a, ha, ha‚āÄ‚ü© : ‚ąÉ a ‚ąą S, a ‚Č† 0 := (hSinf.diff <| Set.finite_singleton 0).nonempty
    96‚Üí  refine ‚ü®(¬∑ - a / 2) '' (S \ {a}) \ S, Set.diff_subset_compl .., ?_, ?_‚ü©
    97‚Üí  -- Since `S` is Sidon and `a ‚Č† 0`, `(S - a / 2) ‚ą© S ‚äá (S \ {a} - a / 2) ‚ą© S` has at most one
    98‚Üí  -- element. In particular, `#A = #(S \ {a} - a / 2) = #S = ūĚĒ†` as wanted.
    99‚Üí  ¬∑ rw [mk_diff_eq_left_of_finite' ((hSinf.diff <| Set.finite_singleton _).image
   100‚Üí      sub_left_injective.injOn)]
   101‚Üí    ¬∑ simp [Cardinal.mk_image_eq sub_left_injective, *]
   102‚Üí    ¬∑ refine Set.Subsingleton.finite ?_
   103‚Üí      rintro _ ‚ü®‚ü®x, ‚ü®hx, -‚ü©, rfl‚ü©, hxa‚ü© _ ‚ü®‚ü®y, ‚ü®hy, -‚ü©, rfl‚ü©, hya‚ü©
   104‚Üí      obtain rfl : x = y := by
   105‚Üí        simpa [ha‚āÄ, eq_comm (b := x - _)] using hS _ hya _ hxa _ hx _ hy (by ring)
   106‚Üí      rfl
   107‚Üí  -- Since `S` is Sidon, `S \ {a} + S \ {a} - a ‚äá A + A` is disjoint from `S`, as wanted.
   108‚Üí  ¬∑ rintro _ ‚ü®‚ü®x, ‚ü®hx, hxa‚ü©, rfl‚ü©, -‚ü© _ ‚ü®‚ü®y, ‚ü®hy, hya‚ü©, rfl‚ü©, -‚ü© hxy
   109‚Üí    have := hS _ hx _ ha _ hy _ hxy (by ring)
   110‚Üí    simp_all
   111‚Üí
   112‚Üíend Erdos949
