    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# Gromov's theorem on groups of polynomial growth
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:*
    23‚Üí - [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gromov%27s_theorem_on_groups_of_polynomial_growth)
    24‚Üí-/
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí/-
    27‚ÜíNote: this was obtained in work with Kasia Jankiewicz and Catherine Pfaff, and using
    28‚ÜíClaude 4.0 Sonnet: https://claude.ai/share/918bb269-bd28-4c09-b84e-cab579c836e8
    29‚Üí-/
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üínamespace GromovPolynomialGrowth
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üíopen Filter
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üívariable {G : Type*} [Group G]
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí/-- The `CayleyBall` is the ball of radius `n` in the Cayley graph of a group `G` with generating
    38‚Üí    set `S`. -/
    39‚Üídef CayleyBall (S : Set G) (n : ‚Ąē) : Set G :=
    40‚Üí  {g : G | ‚ąÉ (l : List G), l.length ‚Č§ n ‚ąß (‚ąÄ s ‚ąą l, s ‚ąą S ‚ą® s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ąą S) ‚ąß l.prod = g}
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí@[category API, AMS 20]
    43‚Üítheorem cayleyBall_zero (S : Set G) :
    44‚Üí    CayleyBall S 0 = {1} := by simp [CayleyBall]
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí@[category API, AMS 20]
    47‚Üílemma cayleyBall_finite {S : Set G} (hS : S.Finite) (n : ‚Ąē) : (CayleyBall S n).Finite := by
    48‚Üí  have hu : (S ‚ą™ (¬∑)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ '' S).Finite := hS.union (by simpa using hS.preimage inv_injective.injOn)
    49‚Üí  have hf (m : ‚Ąē) : {f : Fin m ‚Üí G | ‚ąÄ i, f i ‚ąą S ‚ą® (f i)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ąą S}.Finite := by
    50‚Üí    simpa using Set.Finite.pi' fun _ ‚Ü¶ hu
    51‚Üí  have : {l : List G | l.length ‚Č§ n ‚ąß ‚ąÄ s ‚ąą l, s ‚ąą S ‚ą® s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ąą S}.Finite :=
    52‚Üí    ((Set.finite_le_nat n).biUnion fun m _ ‚Ü¶ (hf m).image List.ofFn).subset
    53‚Üí      fun l ‚ü®hl, hlS‚ü© ‚Ü¶ Set.mem_biUnion hl ‚ü®fun i ‚Ü¶ l[i], by aesop‚ü©
    54‚Üí  exact (this.image List.prod).subset fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ by aesop (add simp [CayleyBall])
    55‚Üí
    56‚Üí/-- The `GrowthFunction` of a group `G` with respect to a set `S` counts the number
    57‚Üí    of group elements that can be reached by words of length at most `n` in `S`. -/
    58‚Üínoncomputable def GrowthFunction (S : Set G) (n : ‚Ąē) : ‚Ąē :=
    59‚Üí  (CayleyBall S n).ncard
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí@[category API, AMS 20]
    62‚Üítheorem growthFunction_zero (S : Set G) :
    63‚Üí    GrowthFunction S 0 = 1 := by
    64‚Üí  simp [GrowthFunction, CayleyBall]
    65‚Üí
    66‚Üí-- Basic properties of CayleyBall and GrowthFunction (Claude generated statements, human proofs)
    67‚Üí
    68‚Üí/-- The identity is always in the Cayley ball of radius n for any $n ‚Č• 0$. -/
    69‚Üí@[category API, AMS 20]
    70‚Üílemma one_mem_cayleyBall (S : Set G) (n : ‚Ąē) :
    71‚Üí    1 ‚ąą CayleyBall S n := by
    72‚Üí  simp only [CayleyBall, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    73‚Üí  use ‚ąÖ
    74‚Üí  simp
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/-- The Cayley ball is monotonic in radius. -/
    77‚Üí@[category API, AMS 20]
    78‚Üílemma cayleyBall_monotone (S : Set G) {m n : ‚Ąē} (h : m ‚Č§ n) :
    79‚Üí    CayleyBall S m ‚äÜ CayleyBall S n := by
    80‚Üí  simp only [CayleyBall, Set.setOf_subset_setOf, forall_exists_index, and_imp]
    81‚Üí  exact fun g l lLength LSubS lProdG ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®l, by linarith, LSubS, lProdG‚ü©
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí/-- Closure property: if g, h ‚ąą CayleyBall S m, CayleyBall S n respectively,
    84‚Üí    then gh ‚ąą CayleyBall S (m + n). -/
    85‚Üí@[category API, AMS 20]
    86‚Üílemma cayleyBall_mul (S : Set G) {g h : G} {m n : ‚Ąē}
    87‚Üí    (hg : g ‚ąą CayleyBall S m) (hh : h ‚ąą CayleyBall S n) :
    88‚Üí    g * h ‚ąą CayleyBall S (m + n) := by
    89‚Üí  simp only [CayleyBall, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hg hh ‚äĘ
    90‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®lg, lgLength, lgSubS, lgProd‚ü© := hg
    91‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®lh, lhLength, lhSubS, lhProd‚ü© := hh
    92‚Üí  refine ‚ü®lg ++ lh, ?_, ?_, by simp [lhProd, lgProd]‚ü©
    93‚Üí  ¬∑ simp only [List.length_append]
    94‚Üí    linarith
    95‚Üí  ¬∑ intro s sIn
    96‚Üí    simp only [List.mem_append] at sIn
    97‚Üí    cases sIn with
    98‚Üí    | inl h => simp [lgSubS s h]
    99‚Üí    | inr h => simp [lhSubS s h]
   100‚Üí
   101‚Üí/-- If `g ‚ąą CayleyBall S n`, then `g‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ąą CayleyBall S n`. -/
   102‚Üí@[category API, AMS 20]
   103‚Üílemma cayleyBall_inv (S : Set G) {g : G} {n : ‚Ąē}
   104‚Üí    (hg : g ‚ąą CayleyBall S n) :
   105‚Üí    g‚ĀĽ¬Ļ ‚ąą CayleyBall S n := by
   106‚Üí  simp only [CayleyBall, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hg ‚äĘ
   107‚Üí  obtain ‚ü®lg, lgLength, lgSubS, lgProd‚ü© := hg
   108‚Üí  refine ‚ü®lg.reverse.map (¬∑‚ĀĽ¬Ļ), by simp [lgLength], ?_,
   109‚Üí    by simp [List.prod_inv_reverse, lgProd.symm]‚ü©
   110‚Üí  intro s sIn
   111‚Üí  simp only [List.map_reverse, List.mem_reverse, List.mem_map, inv_involutive,
   112‚Üí    Function.Involutive.exists_mem_and_apply_eq_iff] at sIn
   113‚Üí  have := lgSubS s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ sIn
   114‚Üí  simp only [inv_inv] at this
   115‚Üí  exact this.symm
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí-- end of Claude generated statements
   118‚Üí
   119‚Üí@[category API, AMS 20]
   120‚Üílemma mem_cayleyBall_one_of_mem {S : Set G} {g : G} (hg : g ‚ąą S) : g ‚ąą CayleyBall S 1 :=
   121‚Üí  ‚ü®[g], by simp_all‚ü©
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí@[category API, AMS 20]
   124‚Üílemma exists_cayleyBall_mem_of_closure_eq_top {S : Set G} (h : Subgroup.closure S = ‚ä§) (g : G) :
   125‚Üí    ‚ąÉ n, g ‚ąą CayleyBall S n := by
   126‚Üí  induction h ‚Ėł Subgroup.mem_top g using Subgroup.closure_induction with
   127‚Üí  | mem => exact ‚ü®1, mem_cayleyBall_one_of_mem ‚ÄĻ_‚Äļ‚ü©
   128‚Üí  | one => exact ‚ü®0, one_mem_cayleyBall ..‚ü©
   129‚Üí  | mul _ _ _ _ hg‚āĀ hg‚āā =>
   130‚Üí    obtain ‚ü®n‚āĀ, hn‚āĀ‚ü© := hg‚āĀ
   131‚Üí    obtain ‚ü®n‚āā, hn‚āā‚ü© := hg‚āā
   132‚Üí    exact ‚ü®n‚āĀ + n‚āā, cayleyBall_mul S hn‚āĀ hn‚āā‚ü©
   133‚Üí  | inv _ _ hg =>
   134‚Üí    obtain ‚ü®n, hn‚ü© := hg
   135‚Üí    exact ‚ü®n, cayleyBall_inv S hn‚ü©
   136‚Üí
   137‚Üí/-- In an infinite group, the growth function with respect to a finite generating set
   138‚Üíis unbounded. -/
   139‚Üí@[category API, AMS 20]
   140‚Üítheorem tendsto_atTop_growthFunction_of_infinite [Infinite G] {S : Set G} (hS : S.Finite)
   141‚Üí    (h : Subgroup.closure S = ‚ä§) : atTop.Tendsto (GrowthFunction S) atTop := by
   142‚Üí  delta GrowthFunction
   143‚Üí  have (n : ‚Ąē) : Fintype (CayleyBall S n) := (cayleyBall_finite hS n).fintype
   144‚Üí  apply ((Finset.tendsto_card_atTop).comp (f := fun n ‚Ü¶ (CayleyBall S n).toFinset) ?_).congr
   145‚Üí    (by simp [Set.ncard_eq_toFinset_card'])
   146‚Üí  apply tendsto_atTop_atTop_of_monotone fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ by simpa using cayleyBall_monotone S
   147‚Üí  intro A
   148‚Üí  obtain rfl | hA := A.eq_empty_or_nonempty
   149‚Üí  ¬∑ aesop
   150‚Üí  ¬∑ choose n hn using fun (a : A) ‚Ü¶ exists_cayleyBall_mem_of_closure_eq_top h a
   151‚Üí    let N : ‚Ąē := (Set.range n).toFinset.max' (by simp [hA])
   152‚Üí    refine ‚ü®N, fun a ha ‚Ü¶ ?_‚ü©
   153‚Üí    simpa using cayleyBall_monotone S (Finset.le_max' _ _ (by aesop)) (hn ‚ü®a, ha‚ü©)
   154‚Üí
   155‚Üí/-- Infinite groups do not satisfy polynomial growth over `‚Ąē` for any degree `d` because when
   156‚Üí`d = 0` this reduces to the unbounded nature of `growthFunction` while `n = 0` works when `d ‚Č† 0`.
   157‚ÜíThus a finitely-generated infinite nilpotent group would be a counter-example to
   158‚ÜíGromov's theorem when quantifying over all of `‚Ąē`, and so `n = 0` should be excluded. -/
   159‚Üí@[category test, AMS 20]
   160‚Üítheorem growthFunction_not_polynomial_of_infinite [Infinite G] {S : Set G} (hS : S.Finite)
   161‚Üí    (h : Subgroup.closure S = ‚ä§) {C : ‚ĄĚ} (d : ‚Ąē) :
   162‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (n : ‚Ąē), C * n ^ d < GrowthFunction S n := by
   163‚Üí  by_cases hd : d = 0
   164‚Üí  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®n, _, hn‚ü© := exists_lt_of_tendsto_atTop (tendsto_atTop_growthFunction_of_infinite hS h)
   165‚Üí      0 ‚ĆąC‚ĆČ‚āä
   166‚Üí    use n
   167‚Üí    grw [Nat.le_ceil C]
   168‚Üí    simpa [hd] using mod_cast hn
   169‚Üí  ¬∑ exact ‚ü®0, by simp [hd, growthFunction_zero]‚ü©
   170‚Üí
   171‚Üívariable (G)
   172‚Üí
